Pattern_raw_name: spec.md → plan.md → tasks.md → implement (Spec-Kit document-handoff chain)
How_implemented: Each /growthkit.* command writes one artifact under specs/###-campaign-name/ that the next command reads: create-new-feature.sh mkdirs the campaign folder + checklists/ + assets/ and emits a JSON object {spec_file, plan_file, tasks_file, branch_name}; specify writes spec.md, plan reads spec.md and writes plan.md (+research.md/data-model.md/quickstart.md/contracts/), tasks reads plan.md and writes tasks.md, implement reads tasks.md+plan.md+data-model+contracts+research+quickstart and executes. The written spec is the durable source of truth — 'No implementation without an approved specification' (Constitution Principle I).

A single governance doc of 7 NON-NEGOTIABLE principles ('Constitution Supremacy: principles win' over ad-hoc tactics) that the plan command must validate via explicit 'Constitution Check Gates' — Mandatory gates (spec exists, growth type identified, audience specific, viral loop defined, k-factor target, metrics defined, pilot scoped) MUST pass before Phase 0 research and BLOCK implementation if unjustified; Advisory gates re-checked after Phase 1. Carries an embedded machine-readable SYNC IMPACT REPORT header tracking version bumps + which templates each amendment touched, and a semver amendment process (MAJOR/MINOR/PATCH).

Pattern_raw_name: Metrics-first success gates (Growth Metrics Dashboard 2025 benchmarks)
How_implemented: Principle IV bakes a benchmark table into the constitution (Acceptance Rate >30%, Time-to-Wow <5min, Time-to-Trust <7d, k-factor >1.1, NRR 106-120%, Time-to-First-Share <24h) and forbids vanity metrics without a paired activation metric; every spec MUST declare primary+activation+engagement metrics, and these targets become the go/no-go thresholds (k-factor>0.8 min, engagement>5%) the implement command checks at the pilot validation checkpoint. Metrics function as the durable success contract the whole chain executes against.

A bash script heredoc-writes a fixed CLAUDE.md (and .cursor/README.md, .windsurf/README.md) that acts as the root identity/index file: it lists project structure, 'Key Files to Reference' (constitution + specs/001-*/spec|plan|tasks), the available slash commands, tech stack, the 7 principles, the constitution-check gates, and the 2025 metric benchmarks — a deterministic map pointing agents at where knowledge lives rather than embedding it.

Tier-3 just-in-time knowledge: refs/0_overview.md … refs/7_optimization.md hold the detailed growth research/case studies per workflow phase (1_principles→constitution, 2_define→specify, 3→plan, 4→tasks, 5_writing→optimize, 6/7→claude code). Specs MUST reference refs/1_principles_for_constitution.md; the constitution names refs/ as the 'working reference library' updated quarterly — content is pulled in by stage rather than always-loaded.

Each campaign is a numbered folder specs/###-campaign-name/ (spec/plan/tasks + checklists/ + assets/) on a matching git branch; create-new-feature.sh derives the next ###- number by scanning remote branches, local branches, and existing specs/ dirs for the short-name. Multi-Kit Architecture Standards enforce that this co-exists with spec-kit: separate .growthkit/ dir, growthkit-cli package, /growthkit.* command prefix, GROWTHKIT_CAMPAIGN env var — so the same repo can hold .specify/, .growthkit/, .blogkit/ change-folders side by side (repo actually ships all three).

Root CLAUDE.md describes the 'Tier 4 skill' identity, 3-layer design (directive / orchestration / execution), repo topology (dual origin/aimh remotes, release-on-private-first), security rules (validate_url SSRF gate, creds in ~/.config/claude-seo). AGENTS.md is the cross-harness entry doc; .claude-plugin/plugin.json is the machine-readable manifest declaring name/version/description and the 25-skill/18-agent count.

Pattern_raw_name: Progressive Disclosure routing ('Read SKILL.md for routing, load references on demand')
How_implemented: The /seo orchestrator SKILL.md holds only a routing table (command -> sub-skill); each sub-skill (e.g. seo-technical) keeps heavy domain knowledge in a references/ subfolder (e.g. references/agent-friendly-pages.md) loaded just-in-time. AGENTS.md states the explicit principle: 'Progressive Disclosure: Read SKILL.md for routing, load references on demand.' Frontmatter description is the auto-invocation/trigger surface; full content drills in only when matched.

seo-audit agent writes a typed JSON envelope (audit-data.json) plus per-category files in findings/ to {domain}-audit/; downstream `python3 scripts/google_report.py` reads that envelope to render an A4 PDF (WeasyPrint + matplotlib charts at 200 DPI). The JSON is the doc-as-baton handed from analysis step to report step — a structured payload, not free text.

Drift monitoring stores SEO snapshots in SQLite at ~/.cache/claude-seo/drift/baselines.db (tables: baselines, comparisons). A baseline captures 13 fields (title, meta, canonical, robots, H1-H3, JSON-LD, OG, CWV, status, plus SHA-256 hashes of HTML+schema). `compare` fetches current state, normalizes the URL (lowercase scheme/host, strip default ports, sort query params, drop UTM, strip trailing slash), and applies 17 rules across CRITICAL/WARNING/INFO to detect regressions vs the most recent baseline — persistent memory outside the context window, used as a cross-session continuity record of a site's SEO state.

seo/SKILL.md contains a Quick Reference markdown table mapping each /seo subcommand to the sub-skill that handles it — an explicit map of where each capability lives, used to dispatch without any vector store.

Per CLAUDE.md and README, each recommendation carries 'the first-principle observation it rests on' plus explicit failure-mode checks and a success metric (observation basis, system dependencies, success metrics). Findings are graded by a 0-100 SEO Health Score weighted across 7 categories with severity tied to falsifiability checks — claims are source/observation-bound rather than asserted.

A single foundation skill (product-marketing) creates one canonical context file at .agents/product-marketing.md capturing 12 sections (positioning, ICP, personas, JTBD four-forces, competitive landscape, differentiation, objections, verbatim customer language, brand voice, proof points, goals). Every other skill opens with a 'Check for product marketing context first' step that reads this file (with .claude/ and legacy product-marketing-context.md fallbacks) before asking questions, so foundational context is written once and re-read by all 44 other skills. It is a written source-of-truth artifact agents execute against, scaffolded by a dedicated command into a fixed path.

Pattern_raw_name: AGENTS.md (CLAUDE.md is a one-line pointer to it)
How_implemented: Root AGENTS.md is the agent identity/rules/output-standards doc: repo overview, structure map, frontmatter field-constraint tables, writing-style + tone guidelines, description-field best practices, git/PR checklist, and tool-integration routing. CLAUDE.md contains literally the single line 'AGENTS.md' to redirect Claude Code to the cross-agent doc — deliberate single-source-of-truth so Claude-specific and generic guidance never diverge.

REGISTRY.md is an explicit index table (51 tools x API/MCP/CLI/SDK columns) that agents read to discover capabilities, then drill into the just-in-time per-tool integration guide (tools/integrations/<tool>.md) only for the tool actually needed. Skills cite tools by name; the agent loads the full integration doc lazily rather than carrying all 51 in context.

Pattern_raw_name: Dynamic context injection via !`command` (Claude Code-only enhancement note)
How_implemented: AGENTS.md documents (but deliberately keeps OUT of cross-agent SKILL.md files) a Claude Code pattern: placing !`cat .agents/product-marketing.md 2>/dev/null || echo "...ask the user"` at the top of a skill body so Claude Code executes the shell and injects the product context inline at invocation — the model sees the file contents, not a 'go read this file' instruction. Same trick suggested for injecting today's date / current git branch / recent commits for recency-sensitive skills.

Pattern_raw_name: market/SKILL.md main orchestrator + Routing Logic table
How_implemented: A single root orchestrator SKILL.md acts as identity + command map + output standards: a 15-row `/market <command>` table maps each command to its sub-skill file and named output artifact (MARKETING-AUDIT.md, COMPETITOR-REPORT.md, etc.), plus global Output Standards (actionable/prioritized/revenue-focused/example-driven/client-ready) and a Business Context Detection rubric (6 business types). Functions as the always-loaded map of where each capability and its output live.

Skills coordinate purely through named Markdown artifacts written to the current working directory and re-read by later skills by literal filename. market-audit's 'Cross-Skill Integration' section: if COMPETITOR-REPORT.md or BRAND-VOICE.md exists in cwd, fold its findings in. market-report-pdf 'Step 1: Collect All Available Data' enumerates 9 expected files (MARKETING-AUDIT.md, LANDING-CRO.md, SEO-AUDIT.md, BRAND-VOICE.md, COMPETITOR-ANALYSIS.md, FUNNEL-ANALYSIS.md, ...) and assembles them into the PDF — a doc-as-baton chain keyed on file existence, no DB/index.

Pattern_raw_name: Phase-1 Discovery shared payload (business type + page map + fetched content)
How_implemented: Before fan-out, the orchestrator runs a Discovery phase that WebFetches homepage + up to 5 interior pages, classifies the business into 1 of 6 types via a detection-signals table, and builds a 'page map' of site architecture. These three objects (business type, page map, raw fetched content) are explicitly described as passed to every subagent so all 5 analyze the same shared context — an informal typed context packet handed to the fan-out workers.

Root /CLAUDE.md is the governance/identity doc: hard rules (PRs target dev not main; stdlib-only no-LLM Python tools; 'no silent chaining' for orchestrators), output standards (5-7 sourced references with explicit confidence levels, 125-char quote caps, persona voice differentiation), a domain coverage table, and an explicit 'Always-Loaded Facts' block (no build system, current version, multi-platform list, reference-canon rule). Each domain folder also carries its own CLAUDE.md (e.g. product-team/CLAUDE.md states 'all product Python tools must' be CLI-first/stdlib-only and indexes its skills). GEMINI.md mirrors for the Gemini harness.

Pattern_raw_name: SKILL.md 'Before Starting' context-first checkpoint + company-context.md single source of truth
How_implemented: The SKILL-AUTHORING-STANDARD mandates every SKILL.md open with a 'Before Starting' section: a context-first checkpoint naming WHICH domain context file to read first, plus a three-tier forcing-question pattern (Current State / Goals / Domain-Specific). A paired setup skill (cs-onboard-style) + /cs:setup command run a guided interview that writes a canonical company-context.md that all downstream skills reference before asking anything — preventing redundant discovery and enforcing one strategic-context source of truth. References load on demand to 'keep startup cost zero' (progressive disclosure).

Self-contained plugin (.claude-plugin/, agents/, commands/, hooks/, skills/handoff/). Writes a markdown handoff-XXXXXX.md to a configured location (.handoff/ or OS temp) with a strict 5-section template: Goal of next session / State of play / Open decisions / Skills to use (3-5, capped) / Artifacts. hooks.json wires SessionStart→session_start.py (scans the save dir for the latest handoff within a retention window and injects it as <handoff_from_previous_session> context, framed as background NOT directives) and SessionEnd→session_end.py (non-blocking reminder if no handoff < 30 min old). Both toggle via HANDOFF_SESSIONSTART/SESSIONEND env flags. Discipline: reference artifacts by path/commit hash, never duplicate diffs/PRDs.

Pattern_raw_name: self-improving-agent three-tier memory (MEMORY.md / topic files / CLAUDE.md)
How_implemented: Three-tier memory: (1) MEMORY.md project-level auto-capture written continuously during sessions, (2) overflow topic files under ~/.claude/projects/<path>/memory/*.md, (3) CLAUDE.md curated enforced rules. Promotion lifecycle: a pattern recurs 2-3x → /si:review (memory-analyst agent) flags it as a promotion candidate and identifies consolidation/dedup groups → human approves → /si:promote graduates it to CLAUDE.md or rules/ → the MEMORY.md entry is REMOVED to free space. Explicit tiering of transient (MEMORY.md) vs permanent (CLAUDE.md) with periodic reconciliation; an end-of-session capture loop feeds it.

Authoring standard requires every reference doc cite 5-7 authoritative 'canon' sources (peer-reviewed/industry-standard/documented framework) each with an explicit confidence level, and every finding in skill output be tagged 🟢 verified / 🟡 medium / 🔴 assumed. Bottom-line-first 'Communication Standard' baked into the SKILL.md template.

Orchestration Pattern C passes a structured handoff summary between personas (decisions made / artifacts created / open questions) as a document-as-baton — e.g. 'Switching to Phase 2. Load growth-marketer persona and launch-strategy skill.' Explicitly NO typed payload/context_packet schema is defined; coordination relies on disciplined summary format, so this is CM-08 doc-handoff, only weakly CM-20.

Pattern_raw_name: Markdown memory vault (SOUL.md / USER.md / MEMORY.md / daily/YYYY-MM-DD.md)
How_implemented: Local markdown files are the single source of truth — no DB for the canonical store. Tier-1 always-loaded identity = SOUL.md (personality/values/boundaries) + USER.md (profile, account IDs, prefs); Tier-2 curated KB = MEMORY.md with fixed sections (Decisions / Lessons Learned / Important Facts / Active Projects / Upcoming Events / Preferences Confirmed); Tier-3 append-only granular logs = daily/YYYY-MM-DD.md. A curation model promotes only material items from daily logs up into MEMORY.md during heartbeats — a living LLM-maintained KB plus end-of-session capture.

SOUL.md is a non-negotiable identity+values doc loaded every session: four 'core values' pillars (Be Genuinely Helpful, Have Opinions, Be Resourceful Before Asking, Earn Trust Through Competence) plus a proactivity spectrum rule (internally bold: read/search/organize without asking; externally cautious: ask before any outbound/public action). Functions as an anti-drift governance gate the agent re-reads on SessionStart.

A SessionStart hook script reads SOUL.md + USER.md + MEMORY.md + recent daily-log entries and injects them into the new conversation automatically, so identity and recent decisions persist with no user prompting. The daily log doubles as a HANDOFF-style continuity journal across sessions.

Pattern_raw_name: PreCompact + SessionEnd memory flush (pre-compact-flush.py / session-end-flush.py → memory_flush.py)
How_implemented: Both hooks share one pattern: extract conversation turns → write to a temp file → spawn a background memory_flush.py via the Claude Agent SDK. The flush sub-agent runs with allowed_tools=[] (pure reasoning, no tool access = a context firewall) to distill only noteworthy decisions/lessons/facts into bullet summaries appended to the daily log, or writes 'FLUSH_OK' if nothing matters. Recursion guard: every SDK invocation sets os.environ['CLAUDE_INVOKED_BY']; the hooks skip if it's set so a flush can't trigger another flush.

Pattern_raw_name: Hybrid vector+keyword memory search (db.py / embeddings.py / memory_index.py / memory_search.py)
How_implemented: Memory markdown is chunked into ~400-token overlapping chunks, embedded locally by FastEmbed ONNX (all-MiniLM-L6-v2, 384-dim, no external API), stored in SQLite via sqlite-vec (or Postgres pgvector on VPS). Keyword side uses SQLite FTS5 (or Postgres tsvector+GIN). Retrieval fuses scores at 0.7 vector + 0.3 keyword. Incremental indexing re-processes only changed files. The store is a search index over the markdown, not the source of truth — DB-backed retrieval layered on a markdown vault.

Cron/Task-Scheduler runs heartbeat.py every 30 min in active hours. Two-phase: Python pre-loads data from all enabled integrations BEFORE invoking Claude (cheap, deterministic gather), then Claude reasons over the preloaded context. State diffing compares a freshly-built snapshot against the prior run's snapshot stored in .claude/data/state/heartbeat-state.json so notifications fire only on change — ~$0.05/run vs ~$0.38 with live MCP tool calls.

On first run BOOTSTRAP.md drives a one-question-at-a-time interview (name/email, timezone, role+projects, primary use case, comms style, active hours, integrations, proactivity boundaries) and writes the answers into USER.md, SOUL.md (comms style + proactive rules), HEARTBEAT.md (checks only for configured integrations) and the first daily log — then runs `rm Memory/BOOTSTRAP.md` to remove itself so it never re-fires. Context-scaffolding setup that populates the always-loaded files then disappears.

block-secrets.py is a PreToolUse hook intercepting Read/Bash/Grep/Edit/Write/Glob: a path blocklist (.env, .pem, .key, credentials.json, google_token.json, SSH keys) plus command sanitization (blocks cat .env, printenv, echo $TOKEN, python -c os.environ, and scripts that exfiltrate secrets to stdout; recurses into subshells). sanitize.py wraps external text (emails/API responses) in XML trust boundaries + markdown-escaping to defend against injection.

Pattern_raw_name: Generated PRD as durable build spec (.agent/plans/second-brain-prd.md)
How_implemented: The skill's whole output is a written, phased PRD (9 phases, each with dependencies/complexity/personalization notes) that the user then executes against — the spec is the durable context artifact. The skill chains read-requirements → load-architecture-reference → web-research → generate-PRD as a document handoff chain where each step's artifact feeds the next.

Pattern_raw_name: Layered identity files: SOUL.md / CRITICAL_FACTS.md (~120 tokens) / CORE_VALUES.md
How_implemented: L0 of the context engine reads three tiny always-loaded files: SOUL.md (or About Me.md) for identity/comms style, CRITICAL_FACTS.md (~120 tokens: timezone, role, manager, location, company), and CORE_VALUES.md/Values.md for decision principles. Total L0 budget ~170 tokens. CRITICAL_FACTS is explicitly token-budgeted as the always-loaded core preamble.

Single command loads vault state in four ascending token tiers, 'start light, go deeper only as needed': L0 identity (~170 tok: SOUL/CRITICAL_FACTS/CORE_VALUES); L1 navigation (~1-2K: index.md catalog + last 10 log entries); L2 current state (~2-5K: Home/Dashboard, last 3 daily notes, active kanban boards, prior session digests); L3 deep context (on-demand 5-20K: active project notes, references, key contacts loaded only when a question requires). L0-L2 shown without loading L3 so the user can resume immediately.

Pattern_raw_name: _CLAUDE.md vault operating manual + index.md page catalog
How_implemented: Vault root holds _CLAUDE.md (operating manual) and index.md ('Page catalog (read first by Claude)'). A SessionStart hook (load_vault_context.py) injects _CLAUDE.md once per session when cwd is inside the vault, prints absolute paths for _CLAUDE.md/index.md/log.md, and explicitly instructs Claude NOT to run ls/Glob/Bash discovery — replacing filesystem exploration with a curated map.

Pattern_raw_name: Bi-temporal facts via timeline: frontmatter array
How_implemented: Rather than overwriting a fact, the system APPENDS to a `timeline:` frontmatter array preserving both event time (when something was true) and transaction time (when the vault learned it). Enables historical queries and reflective thinking ('You believed X on Tuesday; after ingesting Y on Wednesday you shifted to Z') with a full audit trail. Reinforced by inline recency markers '(as of 2026-04, source.com)' and confidence levels (stated|high|medium|speculation).

/obsidian-ingest spawns parallel subagents (entities/concepts/projects/contradictions) that SEARCH then REWRITE 5-15 existing pages — merging new info into integrated biographies/concepts rather than timestamped additions. Explicit success metric: 'If an ingest only creates new pages and doesn't rewrite anything, it wasn't deep enough.' Cross-source patterns trigger automatic synthesis pages; stale claims replaced with history preserved.

Four parallel subagents (claims/entity/decisions/source-freshness) scan for conflicts, then score each across three axes — temporal (which source newer), authority (peer-reviewed > blog > transcript), nature (genuine contradiction vs intentional evolution). Three resolution paths: clear winner -> rewrite outdated page + add ## History section; ambiguous -> create wiki/decisions/Conflict — Topic.md with status: open; evolution -> update + preserve history. Compares raw/ source dates against wiki/ publication dates for staleness ordering.

Every answer also writes a vault update (Two-Output Rule). A propagation matrix mandates every write cascade to 3-5 related locations: e.g. project creation triggers board updates + daily-note links + index modifications. Hard rule 'Never create in isolation' and 'No orphans — every note linked from boards/daily note.' Synthesis Hook auto-creates a concept page when a pattern appears across 3+ sources.

Append-only operation logs (per-day Logs/YYYY-MM-DD.md or monolithic log.md) record every save/ingest/reconcile with counts ('X synthesis pages created, Y orphans rescued, Z connections found') as a full audit trail; the context engine reads the last 10 entries at L1 to restore session continuity.

Mandatory [[wikilinks]] for every person/project/idea/decision/concept; missing targets get stub notes. Retrieval is markdown traversal of the index.md catalog + wikilink graph (no vector DB); a /obsidian-retrieval-eval command exists to evaluate retrieval quality on this file-based graph.

Pattern_raw_name: raw/ immutable source material vs wiki/ derived knowledge
How_implemented: Vault separates raw/ (immutable ingested source material, dated) from wiki/ (entities/concepts/projects/daily/logs/reviews/tasks/decisions — derived, rewritable). Reconcile compares raw/ dates against wiki/ publication dates to establish authority/freshness; raw is the citation substrate, wiki is the living distillation.

Pattern_raw_name: CLAUDE.md as orientation+dispatcher with a load-on-demand 'Reference map'
How_implemented: Root CLAUDE.md deliberately stays small ('orientation, not the implementation spec'): it holds North Star, the two organizational axes, cross-cutting invariants, and a 'Reference map (load on demand)' table that maps 'When you're working on X' -> 'Read first <doc>' (e.g. retrieval work -> docs/architecture/RETRIEVAL.md; per-file detail -> docs/architecture/KEY_FILES.md). The per-file index was deliberately moved OUT to keep CLAUDE.md loadable every session; a CI guard (scripts/check-key-files-current-state.sh, a CLAUDE.md size cap) fails the build if it bloats. Explicit Tier-1(root)/Tier-2(index)/Tier-3(detail) split, mirrored in the skill layer (thin RESOLVER.md router + fat SKILL.md on demand).

Pattern_raw_name: Markdown brain repo as system-of-record, synced into Postgres/PGLite (two engines, one contract)
How_implemented: git-versioned markdown files (people/, companies/, deals/, concepts/, ideas/, projects/, yc/) are the human-curated source of truth; `gbrain sync` ingests them into a DB. Pluggable engine via src/core/engine-factory.ts dynamically imports 'pglite' (embedded Postgres-on-WASM, zero-config default) or 'postgres' (Supabase + pgvector). gbrain.yml splits db_tracked dirs (git-versioned, hand-edited) from db_only dirs (bulk machine-generated media/transcripts, auto-gitignored, restorable from DB via `gbrain export --restore-only`). The 90 shared ops in src/core/operations.ts generate BOTH the CLI and the MCP server from one contract.

Pattern_raw_name: Self-wiring typed-edge knowledge graph (links table) + graph-query traversal
How_implemented: Postgres `links` table stores typed directed edges (link_type: attended/works_at/invested_in/founded/advises/mentions/source) between page IDs, plus provenance (link_source open kebab tag, origin_page_id, origin_field, resolution_type qualified/unqualified). Edges are extracted with ZERO LLM calls: an auto-link post-hook on every put_page parses [Name](people/slug) refs and infers relationship type; `gbrain extract links` backfills. Agents query via `gbrain graph-query <slug> --type <link_type> --depth N --direction in|out|both` (e.g. 'who works at Acme' = works_at --direction in). 'mentions' edges are excluded from backlink-count for ranking but count toward graph traversal. v0.42.34 'relationalRetrieval' adds a 4th recall arm that resolves a seed entity and walks the typed-edge graph into RRF.

Pattern_raw_name: Hybrid retrieval: pgvector HNSW + TSVECTOR FTS + RRF + source-tier boost, with named cost modes
How_implemented: content_chunks table carries embedding vector(1536) (HNSW cosine index), plus search_vector TSVECTOR (GIN) for BM25-style keyword, plus multimodal embedding_image/embedding_multimodal vector(1024) partial HNSW. `search` = keyword-only (fast, free); `query` = hybrid (vector+keyword+expansion). Reciprocal-rank fusion + small backlink boost. THREE named search modes (conservative/balanced/tokenmax) in src/core/search/mode.ts bundle the cost levers (tokenBudget, LLM multi-query expansion, searchLimit, relationalRetrieval) behind one config key; resolution chain = per-call SearchOpts -> per-key config -> MODE_BUNDLES[mode] -> balanced fallback. A semantic query_cache is keyed by a knobs_hash (folds mode knobs + embedding column/provider) so a tokenmax write can't be served to a conservative read.

Pattern_raw_name: Synthesis-returns-the-answer query skill with citation propagation + explicit gap flags
How_implemented: skills/query/SKILL.md guarantees every answer is grounded in brain content, every claim cites a page slug ('According to [Source: people/jane-doe, compiled truth]'), and gaps are stated explicitly ('the brain doesn't have information on X') rather than hallucinated. Enforces a source-precedence ladder (user direct statements > compiled_truth > timeline entries > external) and surfaces conflicts with both citations. Token-budget aware: read search chunks first, only load full page when needed.

Pattern_raw_name: Provenance & citations as an enforced page invariant (Iron Law) + compiled_truth/timeline split
How_implemented: skills/conventions/quality.md makes inline [Source: ...] citations MANDATORY per fact with typed citation formats (User / Meeting / email / Web / X / Synthesis). pages table separates compiled_truth (synthesized, cited assessment) from timeline (raw dated evidence). 'Back-Linking Iron Law': every mention of an entity with a page MUST create a reverse back-link ('an unlinked mention is a broken brain'), enforced by signal-detector on write and audited by the maintain skill. A Notability Gate blocks low-value page creation.

Pattern_raw_name: Living LLM-maintained wiki + autonomous 'dream cycle' consolidation
How_implemented: `gbrain dream` runs an 8-phase nightly cycle: lint -> backlinks -> sync -> synthesize -> extract -> patterns -> embed -> orphans. SYNTHESIZE reads yesterday's transcripts, runs a cheap Haiku 'significance' verdict (cached in dream_verdicts) to skip routine sessions, then fans out one Sonnet subagent per worth-processing transcript to write reflections/originals/people-timeline pages, reverse-rendering DB->markdown on disk. PATTERNS reads recent reflections (lookback_days default 30) and emits pattern pages when >= min_evidence (default 3) reflections support a theme. Idempotent (transcripts keyed by file_path+content_hash), privacy-filtered (exclude_patterns default medical/therapy as word-boundary regex), and spend-capped (cooldown_hours default 12).

Pattern_raw_name: Memory tiering with per-prefix recency decay + orthogonal salience axis
How_implemented: skills/conventions/salience-and-recency.md exposes two orthogonal query knobs: salience ('mattering' — boosts pages by emotional_weight + active takes, no time component) and recency ('age' — boosts recent effective_date with PER-PREFIX decay: concepts/originals/writing are evergreen, daily/media/chat decay aggressively; tunable halflifeDays+coefficient in gbrain.yml or GBRAIN_RECENCY_DECAY). pages table carries effective_date, effective_date_source sentinel, salience_score, salience_touched_at. Auto-detected from query text by an English regex heuristic ('current state -> on, canonical truth -> off'); --explain shows whether 'caller' or 'auto_heuristic' fired.

Pattern_raw_name: Bi-temporal-ish provenance: effective_date vs created/updated_at + cache-generation clock
How_implemented: pages distinguish effective_date (event/valid time, with effective_date_source provenance sentinel: event_date|date|published|filename|fallback) from created_at/updated_at (ingest time) and links_extracted_at (derivation time). A page_generation_clock + monotonic page_generations JSONB gates query_cache invalidation so read-time mutations don't blow the whole cache. Timeline entries are independently dated DATE rows. Not full Graphiti-style valid/invalid intervals, but the valid-time vs ingest-time separation is explicit.

Pattern_raw_name: Tiered-enrichment entity CRM (people/companies as intelligence dossiers)
How_implemented: skills/enrich/SKILL.md creates person/company pages with compiled-truth State + dated timeline + bidirectional back-links, scaling effort by notability into Tier 1 (full) / Tier 2 (medium) / Tier 3 (minimal). Philosophy: 'read like an intelligence dossier, not a LinkedIn scrape' — capture beliefs, what they're building, trajectory, not just facts. Frontmatter declares writes_to: people/ companies/. Brain-first lookup chain (search -> query -> get_page -> external APIs only if brain empty) prevents redundant external enrichment.

Pattern_raw_name: Brain vs Source: two-axis tenancy with 6-tier resolution
How_implemented: Knowledge is addressed on two orthogonal axes: Brain = WHICH DATABASE (host brain + mountable team-published brains, each own DB + access policy, via gbrain mounts add / --brain / GBRAIN_BRAIN_ID / .gbrain-mount dotfile) and Source = WHICH REPO INSIDE THE DB (wiki/gstack/essays; slugs scope per source via --source / GBRAIN_SOURCE / .gbrain-source). Both follow the same 6-tier resolution precedence. Slugs are unique on (source_id, slug) not globally; every read routes through sourceScopeOpts so a missed thread can't leak cross-source.

Pattern_raw_name: Trust boundary as fail-closed context invariant (remote/untrusted vs local CLI)
How_implemented: OperationContext.remote is a REQUIRED type field: ctx.remote===false (trusted local CLI from src/cli.ts) vs ctx.remote!==false (untrusted agent/MCP from src/mcp/server.ts). Anything not strictly false is treated as remote. Security-sensitive ops (file_upload, shell-job submission, subagent submission) tighten on remote. PROTECTED_JOB_NAMES means MCP/agents can OBSERVE jobs but cannot SUBMIT shell/subagent jobs (operator/autopilot submits). The dream synthesizer runs with an explicit allowed_slug_prefixes allow-list (sourced from _brain-filing-rules.json) so even on prompt-injection it can't write outside sanctioned dirs.

Pattern_raw_name: Filing-rules + taxonomy as a shared write-surface contract
How_implemented: skills/_brain-filing-rules.md (+ machine-readable _brain-filing-rules.json) is the single canonical 'where does a page go' doc consulted before every write; _output-rules.md sets durable-artifact quality (deterministic links built from data not LLM-guessed, no slop/preamble, exact-phrasing preservation, <60char titles). The dream synthesizer's write allow-list is literally sourced from _brain-filing-rules.json globs, so editing the JSON is the only way to widen what the autonomous cycle may write.

Each pipeline skill writes a timestamped artifact into a per-project folder ~/.gstack/projects/{SLUG}/{USER}-{BRANCH}-design-{DATETIME}.md. The NEXT skill never receives a file path explicitly: downstream skills (/plan-eng-review, /plan-ceo-review, /plan-design-review) scan ~/.gstack/projects/$SLUG/ in their preflight audit and pick the MOST RECENT *-design-*.md by timestamp. The doc-as-baton is the durable spec; office-hours produces the design doc as its only output, which becomes structured input downstream. /autoplan extends this: each review phase appends its findings/consensus tables/audit trail to the same plan file before the next phase reads it; it also writes a test-plan artifact and aggregates deferred scope into TODOS.md.

A persistent Postgres-backed knowledge base that indexes this repo's code, ~/.gstack curated memory (CEO plans, design docs, retros), and per-session transcripts. Three storage tiers: local PGLite (zero-config, ~30s), Supabase (shared across machines via connection string), or Remote MCP (registers URL+bearer token in Claude Code MCP config). Each git remote is pinned via a worktree-local ~/.gbrain-source file so a different branch/repo cannot falsely claim 'indexed'. Agents query via gbrain CLI / mcp__gbrain__* tools: search (semantic), query (conversational), code-def/code-refs/code-callers (symbol graph). Per-repo trust tiers read-write/read-only/deny gate writes. Syncs incrementally on every skill start; /sync-gbrain forces refresh, secret-scans before any push to artifacts repos.

Every SKILL.md declares a preamble-tier (1-4) in frontmatter. A TS resolver (scripts/resolvers/preamble.ts + 24 generator modules in preamble/) composes a tier-cumulative preamble injected before the skill's own body. Tier1 (browse/benchmark): bash bootstrap, upgrade check, telemetry/proactive prompts, routing injection, brain-health, completion status. Tier2 (investigate/cso/retro/context-save): +full voice directive, context recovery, writing style, completeness section, confusion protocol, continuous checkpoint, context-health, question tuning. Tier3 (autoplan/office-hours/reviews): +repo-mode + search-before-building. Tier4 (ship/review/qa): same as 3. Order is load-bearing: a comment notes 'Opus reads top-to-bottom and absorbs the first pacing directive it hits; reversing order regresses cadence (v1.6.4.0 bug)'. Invalid tier throws at build.

CLAUDE.md is the always-loaded root doc: intent->skill routing table ('Architecture -> /plan-eng-review', 'Bugs -> /investigate', 'Full Pipeline -> /autoplan'), platform-agnostic rule (skills read CLAUDE.md for project config, ask via AskUserQuestion if missing, then PERSIST the answer back into CLAUDE.md for reuse), writing-style policy, and GBrain query preferences. A routing-injection preamble generator offers to append a predefined routing section into the project's CLAUDE.md exactly once per project (gated by HAS_ROUTING/ROUTING_DECLINED flags).

Two append-only JSONL stores per project under ~/.gstack/projects/<slug>/. decisions.jsonl: durable architectural calls, surfaced by bin/gstack-decision-search BEFORE re-deciding (anti-rework). learnings.jsonl (the /learn skill): patterns/pitfalls/preferences/insights captured during /review, /ship, /investigate runs, each with confidence 1-10, dedup by (key,type) latest-wins. The skill prunes stale entries (uses Glob to detect deleted files referenced in learnings) and contradictory ones, exports to markdown, and the tier-2 preamble auto-surfaces the top 3 learnings if >5 exist at skill start.

Opt-in mode auto-commits WIP as 'WIP:' messages carrying a structured [gstack-context] body (session state embedded in commit bodies). /context-restore reconstructs session state from these crash-recovery commits after a context loss/compaction; /ship filter-squashes the WIP commits before PR to preserve bisect hygiene. A generate-continuous-checkpoint and generate-context-recovery preamble pair wire this into tier-2 skills.

A per-user profile JSON modeling the human: declared + INFERRED dimensions across five axes (scope_appetite, risk_tolerance, detail_preference, autonomy, architecture_care), plus engagement tiers by session count (introduction=0, welcome_back=1-3, regular=4-7, inner_circle=8+). Tracks signals_accumulated, resources_shown (deduped), topics. Nudge eligibility gated by builderSessions>=3 && totalSignals>=5. This profile tunes how skills speak to and pace the user; downstream next-skill recommendations branch on the tier.

Single authoritative operational doc where every fact is a one-line predicate (NAMESPACE.key=value), greppable via `grep '^DEFECT.SOURCE-GREP' CONTEXT.md`. Tiered into Glossary (22+ domain modules with 'Source of truth' file paths), RULESET.* enforceable constraints (CI-checked), WORKTREE.SEAM.* boundary contracts, DEFECT.* failure taxonomy (Symptom/Detect/Fix-forward/Evidence/Root-cause), and an append-only SESSION.* log compressed to predicate refs only. META.RULE.brief-must-cite-doc forces agents to quote predicate IDs verbatim, never paraphrase — an explicit anti-drift governance gate. Both humans and agents load it fresh before dispatch.

Root identity + rules file: directory map (bin/, commands/gsd/, sdk/, agents/, docs/), Node>=22, code-style (CommonJS root / strict-TS ESM SDK), kebab-case naming, test mandate (node:test + vitest, no Jest), PR gate (Closes #NNN + changeset), and security scan requirement (secret-scan.sh, base64-scan.sh, prompt-injection-scan.sh) before release.

Pattern_raw_name: Document-handoff chain: RESEARCH.md → PLAN.md → SUMMARY.md → VERIFICATION.md
How_implemented: Each phase lives under .planning/phases/XX-name/ and the workflow advances by writing artifacts that the next subagent reads: phase-researcher writes {phase}-RESEARCH.md, planner consumes RESEARCH.md + CONTEXT.md + ROADMAP.md + STATE.md and writes PLAN.md, executor consumes PLAN.md and writes SUMMARY.md + STATE.md updates, verifier reads SUMMARY.md adversarially and writes VERIFICATION.md. Subagents return only a compact status block (e.g. '## RESEARCH COMPLETE' with confidence + file path + 3-5 bullets) — the heavy artifact never re-enters orchestrator context (context firewall).

Executor updates STATE.md (plan position, metrics, decisions, blockers) and ROADMAP.md (progress counts) and writes a substantive SUMMARY.md per plan; on resume it receives continuation state via <completed_tasks> in the prompt. ROADMAP.md carries phase goals + requirement IDs that drive prioritization (goal-anchored). SESSION.* lines in CONTEXT.md fold each session's lessons back as predicate refs.

PostToolUse hook (AfterTool on Gemini) reads a per-session budget bridge file (/tmp/claude-ctx-{session_id}.json written by the statusline), and when remaining context drops below 35% (warning) / 25% (critical) injects advisory text as additionalContext into the conversation stream so the AGENT itself becomes context-budget-aware. Debounced (5 tool uses between warnings); at critical it spawns a background subprocess to record a session-state breadcrumb for recovery before exhaustion.

TypeScript SDK (sdk/src/) carries a context-engine + context-truncation pair for token-budget/relevance optimization, plus planning-journal.ts / planning-runtime.ts / plan-parser.ts that persist and parse planning decisions across phases — planning state lives outside the model window in .planning/ files and is re-loaded just-in-time per phase/session runner.

Filesystem IS the knowledge store: 00-inbox (profiles/config/MY-INTEGRATIONS.md/MY-PROFILE.md + archive), 01-daily, 02-personal (strict privacy), 03-professional (watchlists/strategy), 04-projects/<slug>/ (braindumps/, PRDs/, competitive/), 05-knowledge (people CRM, consolidated frameworks, research/), 06-templates. Numbering encodes a synthesis gradient inbox->knowledge. No DB, plain markdown, git-versioned, iCloud-synced. CLAUDE.md's 'Vault Structure' section is the always-loaded map of where each kind of info lives.

Root identity file ('I'm Claude operating as a COG agent ... 200K token budget'). Encodes ALWAYS-APPLY rules: Model Routing table (delegate-to-Sonnet vs keep-in-Opus), Worker Output Rule (<2K inline / >=2K to file+path), Brain-First Knowledge Protocol (read 05-knowledge/people + 04-projects before answering), citation format, Integration Preferences gate, and global response constraints (max 125 chars/quote). Mirrored to AGENTS.md/.cursorrules for other harnesses.

Before answering about people/projects/strategy: (1) read 05-knowledge/people/, (2) check 04-projects/<project>/, (3) synthesize with attribution. Every durable claim cites '[Source: [[path/to/note]] | YYYY-MM-DD | confidence: high|medium|low]' — wikilink + date + 3-level confidence. Profiles store keys/links to source notes and load them just-in-time rather than inlining everything.

Three escalating tiers gated by cross-source mention count: Stub (single mention -> name+role+one-line), Moderate (3+ mentions -> exec snapshot + working style + strengths), Full (8+ mentions OR a direct meeting -> all sections). Profiles are APPEND-ONLY: brief-people-updater agent appends dated, source-cited observations to a Timeline and never overwrites; new profiles cloned from 06-templates/people-profile-template.md (sections: Compiled Truth synthesis + append-only Timeline). Explicit rule 'Don't force a full profile on first encounter. Let it build naturally.' Excludes personal/negative/speculative content so profiles stay shareable.

Workers write results to /tmp/{task-slug}-{context}.md and return ONLY a short status + path (e.g. 'OK: /tmp/research-x.md (5 sources, 23 findings)'). Threshold table: <2K tokens inline, >=2K to file. Lead reads the file and synthesizes — raw worker output never enters the Opus context window. Rationale stated: file I/O is instant, large sequential agent output is slow + token-heavy.

Self-rewriting KB: loop-until-dry scan of braindumps/briefs/notes -> pattern recognition across 5 dimensions (frequency, temporal, domain-correlation, contradiction, cross-cutting) -> frameworks with lifecycle status Emerging->Working->Stable, each carrying Core Principles (evidence trails), Use Cases, Boundaries, Evolution History. Monthly consolidation: marked braindumps flip status captured->consolidated with backlinks; DEDUP against 05-knowledge/consolidated/ (update existing rather than create); superseded insights ARCHIVED to 00-inbox/archive/ with replacement note; major shifts get dedicated evolution docs. Deterministic verifier: every principle must link >=1 source or it is dropped; every in-scope doc must be marked consolidated.

Each skill writes a durable artifact the next reads. generate-prd reads MY-INTEGRATIONS.md + MY-PROFILE.md, writes 9-section PRD to 04-projects/<project>/PRDs/prd-<slug>-YYYY-MM-DD.md; create-user-story, generate-release-notes, update-knowledge-base consume that PRD/project state downstream. The PRD is the source-of-truth artifact; an approval gate blocks publishing to Confluence/Notion until the user OKs.

Shared loop-engineering skill prescribes context discipline inside iterative skills: externalize progress to a vault file as you go; compact+prune (summarize finished passes, discard raw output once facts extracted); isolate sub-agents (give each worker only the needed slice, collect only conclusions). The Update step (step 5 of the 6-step loop) persists progress so the loop survives compaction.

00-inbox/MY-INTEGRATIONS.md tracks each external tool as Active/Disabled/Unknown; CLAUDE.md mandates checking it before any external call (use Active normally, skip Disabled silently, ask+record Unknown). MY-PROFILE.md anchors active projects, name/role and agent_mode (solo|team) which every skill reads to decide whether to fan out workers. Acts as a per-user goal/state anchor driving routing.

Pattern_raw_name: v4 layout: index.yaml + index.md + raw/ + wiki/ (sources/entities/concepts/comparisons/repos/notes/questions + overview/synthesis)
How_implemented: Each research topic is a self-contained directory research-<slug>/. index.yaml is the canonical Tier-2 map (per-source metadata + summaries + uri_source_page pointers); index.md is the Obsidian-navigable mirror; raw/ holds immutable Tier-3 source content; wiki/ holds LLM-synthesized pages. The whole thing is a living, interlinked KB rebuilt incrementally as sources are added — raw notes become an interlinked wiki of source/entity/concept/comparison pages.

Query mode reads sources in escalating layers and STOPS when the question is answered: Layer 1 = index.yaml summary (~2-3 sentences); Layer 1.5 = wiki/sources/<slug>.md extended summary; Layer 2 = uri_highlights (user Readwise annotations); Layer 3 = uri_full raw doc (rarely reached). Retrieval is pure markdown/index traversal — no embeddings, no vector store.

source_writer is the ONLY agent that reads a raw file (in full / chunked >2000 lines); it writes wiki/sources/<slug>.md and returns a compact JSON receipt (output_path, entities_referenced, suggested_new_entities, key_claim_count, quote_count). The raw document never enters the orchestrator or any downstream agent's context — 'makes the raw document's presence invisible to all other agents.' wiki_page_writer / wiki_summary_writer read only source pages, never raw.

Pattern_raw_name: Document-handoff chain via intermediary JSON scratch files
How_implemented: Pipeline passes typed JSON artifacts step-to-step: researchers emit round*-query*.json → dedup_findings.py merges to round*-deduped.json → discovery-final.json → seeds-augmented.json → source-writer-outputs.json → build_index_yaml.py. Each step reads the prior artifact rather than re-deriving. After successful ingest, Step 7 deletes the intermediary JSONs (top-level only), preserving index.yaml/index.md/log.md/raw/wiki.

Every run appends a structured entry to log.md (date, mode, topic, source count, rounds, raw files added, wiki page counts, open questions, skipped). Acts as the cross-session continuity/history record for the research dir.

Every claim sourced from a specific source must wikilink to the raw file or its per-source wiki page (with heading anchor); synthesis-authored (non-sourced) claims must be explicitly prefixed '> Synthesis: ...'. Entity/concept pages carry source_count, mention_count and a confidence level in frontmatter. CONVENTIONS.md enforces this repo-wide.

A user-triggered linter runs 4 mechanical checks (orphan sources, missing hubs for concepts/entities mentioned >=3x without a page, missing comparison pages for entity pairs with >=2 mutual links, broken wikilinks) + 3 LLM subagent checks (stale claims contradicted by newer sources, contradictions, open-questions). Contradictions/open-questions auto-append to their wiki pages; rest flag for user judgment. Read-mostly — never mutates source/entity pages. This is scheduled reconciliation/dedup of the KB.

build_index_md.py is byte-stable; wiki_page_writer skips writes if byte-identical to existing (noop). Append mode merges via build_index_yaml.py --existing-index (dedup by original_path, new wins) and preserves the original created timestamp via --existing-created. User edits protected by <!-- KEEP --> markers so re-runs don't clobber hand-edits — delta-aware self-rewrite.

Pattern_raw_name: Q&A save-back as slim pointer + knowledge doc
How_implemented: In query mode, when an answer cites >=2 sources or the user bookmarks, the knowledge folds into the most appropriate durable doc (entity/concept/comparison/notes page) while wiki/questions/YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>.md stays a <=25-line slim pointer (frontmatter + verbatim Q + one-line why + wikilink to the knowledge doc). Idempotency rule: enrich existing knowledge doc in place rather than duplicate. End-of-query learning folds back into the KB.

Pattern_raw_name: brain/ shared markdown knowledge base + AGENTS.md continuity protocol
How_implemented: brain/ holds always-loaded core context as flat markdown: business-brain.md, active-projects.md, recent-decisions.md, memory.md, constraints.md, identity.md, constitution.md, plus journal/YYYY-MM-DD.md dailies. AGENTS.md is the Tier-1 root identity+rules+output-standards file and prescribes an explicit 5-step session-continuity protocol any new agent (Claude/Cursor/Gemini/Hermes) reads on entry: AGENTS.md -> business-brain.md+memory.md -> data/agent-routes.json -> data/settings.json -> execute then update learnings+audit. This is the cross-session/cross-harness handoff mechanism (no vector DB needed for the read path).

memory_search.py builds a SQLite FTS5 virtual table `memory_fts(id,source,path,title,content,category, tokenize='porter unicode61')` for Porter-stemmed full-text search across brain files, skills, journal and prompts, paired with a `memory_meta` table (timestamps, indexes on source/category for filtering) and an `entities` table. index_brain_files()/index_skills()/index_journal() populate both FTS and meta; search() runs FTS MATCH with ranking and SQLite snippet() highlighting. This is the DB-backed memory store that lives outside any model context window; a /reindex endpoint keeps it fresh.

Pattern_raw_name: Regex entity extraction into entities table
How_implemented: During indexing, regex patterns pull five entity types (person Name-pairs, emails, URLs, acronyms 2+ caps, IP addresses) from each document, dedup them via a set, and store (name,type,context,source,created) in the `entities` table. get_entities() filters by type and returns frequency-ranked results — a lightweight keyword entity layer over the corpus rather than a true typed knowledge graph.

A weekly skill that aggregates all skills' learnings.md, archives recent-decisions.md entries older than 30 days, compresses duplicate lessons into single entries, synthesizes the result into memory.md, prunes each skill's eval score-history.json to the latest 20 entries, and flags contradictions for human review. Activation is multi-trigger: weekly cron, or threshold-based (learnings.md > 600 words, recent-decisions.md >= 30 entries). This is explicit memory consolidation with age-based archival decay.

Six non-negotiable principles (User Sovereignty, Transparency/audit-everything, Cost Consciousness, Adaptive Improvement, Resilient Architecture/multi-agent failover, Consistency&Documentation) plus three prohibitions (no credential exposure, explicit auth for irreversible ops, audit during modifications) and a mandatory 7-step pre-task workflow. Acts as an anti-drift gate read alongside identity.md.

active-projects.md (current status) and recent-decisions.md (rolling decision log) are the live state that drives daily-standup and consolidation; AGENTS.md output standards require each task to append to recent-decisions/audit and refresh learnings, so the state docs are self-evolving deltas rather than static.

Pattern_raw_name: audit/ activity log + git auto-commit of brain/skills
How_implemented: Every agent action is appended to an audit trail (constitution Transparency principle; run_skill records an audit entry on each trigger), and AGENTS.md output standards mandate auto-committing all brain/ and skills/ changes with descriptive messages — git history + audit log together provide provenance and a persistence hook around task execution.

Agents coordinate not by passing messages but by reading/writing a shared key-namespace via mcp__claude-flow__memory_usage. Queen writes status to swarm/queen/status and directives to swarm/shared/royal-directives; coder writes swarm/coder/status and swarm/shared/implementation and retrieves dependencies before acting. This is structured inter-agent context passing through a typed durable store (a blackboard), backed by SQLite (WAL mode) + an HNSW/ONNX-384-dim vector index (AgentDB) for semantic recall.

SessionStart hook runs 'import': loads JSON-backed memory store (.claude-flow/data/auto-memory-store.json) into the session and vectorizes entries into AgentDB (.swarm/memory.db) for semantic search. Stop/SessionEnd runs 'sync': writes accumulated insights back to memory files and curates a MEMORY.md index using graph-aware ordering (an LLM-maintained map of where memory lives). Entries carry id/key/namespace/type/content/tags/timestamps; keys formatted claude-memory:{project}:{file}:{section}. Cross-project namespace 'claude-memories' shares Claude Code memories between repos.

Pattern_raw_name: PreCompact / SessionStart / SessionEnd / Stop hook chain (hook-handler.cjs)
How_implemented: settings.json wires a full lifecycle: PreToolUse(pre-bash), PostToolUse on Write|Edit(post-edit), UserPromptSubmit('route' = auto-task routing), SessionStart(session-restore + memory import), SessionEnd(session-end persist), Stop(memory sync), PreCompact(manual+auto: restore then persist before context compaction), SubagentStop(post-task = capture subagent result back into memory). All route through .claude/helpers/hook-handler.cjs with per-event timeouts. SubagentStop acts as a context firewall point — the subagent's finished work is folded into shared memory rather than left in main context.

The coder agent is instructed to consult docs/SPEC.md (functional scope) and docs/adr/*.md (architectural decisions, binding unless superseded) before any design-affecting code, with an explicit precedence rule: ADRs govern architecture, SPEC governs scope. A written spec + ADR set act as the durable anti-drift governance context agents execute against.
Repo root ships CLAUDE.md (+ CLAUDE.local.md) and AGENTS.md as identity/capability reference and rules loaded as the always-on instruction layer for the orchestration system.

Root identity/rules file defines a fixed bootstrap read-order every session: (1) vault/MEMORY.md long-term memory, (2) today's + yesterday's daily entries, (3) goals/3-weekly.md, (4) .session/handoff.md. Declares a Tier-1 root + Tier-2 hub-index model: each domain has a hub file (MEMORY.md for personal, business/_index.md, projects/_index.md) that is the always-known map; leaf cards loaded just-in-time. Encodes card metadata schema (type/description/tags/status/tier), output constraints (max 125-char quotes, KISS).

A single LLM-maintained long-term memory file read at every bootstrap and rewritten at end-of-session 'if decisions/preferences changed'. dbrain-processor Phase 8-9 updates it by REPLACING outdated info rather than appending (explicit rule), tracking key decisions, pipeline changes, financial events. Functions as a living wiki distilled from the daily capture stream.

A continuity journal refreshed at end-of-session with progress + observations, and read at the next bootstrap. dbrain-processor Phase 10 specifically writes friction signals (errors, empty days), recurring-behavior patterns, and improvement ideas here -> a structured capture loop that survives session compaction/restart.

Each vault card carries a tier (core/active/warm/cold/archive). relevance = max(floor, 1.0 - effective_rate * days_since_access); access_count strengthens via strength = 1 + ln(access_count) so frequently-touched cards decay slower. Domain-specific decay rates (contacts 0.005, daily notes 0.020 -> contacts fade in ~100d, daily in ~25d). GRADUATED RECALL: touching a card promotes it exactly ONE tier (archive->cold->warm->active), never a direct jump. Run as a scheduled decay cycle in process.sh's daily memory-decay step.

Deterministic Python (uv run scripts/graph.py health|fix|backlinks|orphans <vault>) computes a health score targeting >=90/100 from broken-link count (target 0; graph.py fix --apply), orphan count (target <5; connect to hubs), and description coverage (target >=80%). Daily HEALTH workflow regenerates MOC indexes, repairs wikilinks, rescues orphans via backlink queries. Hybrid: scripts handle deterministic graph metrics, the Claude agent handles judgment (classification, dedup decisions).

No vector DB. Retrieval is wikilink traversal from hub/_index.md files plus graph.py backlinks queries. CREATE rule: every new card must immediately link to its domain hub AND >=2 sibling cards or it is an 'orphan' that 'wastes knowledge'. Bidirectional links like [[business/crm/acme-corp|Acme Corp]] are the index.

Consolidation explicitly ordered: deduplicate entities BEFORE link enrichment, 'to prevent creating links to files that get merged'. Dedup uses title fuzzy-match + phrase overlap + tag correspondence (per note-organizer). Always dry-run before --apply.

business/crm/{client}.md cards typed crm/lead/contact with status (business state) + tier (memory rank). dbrain-processor detects business mentions in daily entries and links tasks to the right CRM card with priority assignment; contacts get a slow decay rate (0.005) so relationships persist ~100 days.

Pattern_raw_name: CLAUDE.md vs skills separation (documented convention, not a shipped file)
How_implemented: The repo ships NO CLAUDE.md itself, but the README codifies the architectural rule as its central thesis: 'CLAUDE.md sets global context (who you are, your product, your writing style) and loads every session; skills handle specific tasks and load only when triggered. Identity, style rules, and company context belong in CLAUDE.md. Task-specific workflows belong in skills.' This is the prescriptive root-instruction-file pattern stated as guidance for the consuming project, plus an explicit token-economy rationale (always-loaded root vs on-demand skill).

README 'How Skills Work': skills are markdown files in .claude/skills/ that 'load on demand' — when user input matches a skill's Trigger section, Claude reads the SKILL.md and follows it. Progressive disclosure is via natural-language trigger phrases in each SKILL.md's '## Trigger' section (e.g. 'write a LinkedIn post', 'validate this idea') rather than YAML description matching; the full playbook is only pulled into context once triggered.

Single root AGENTS.md defines the assistant identity ('personal productivity assistant'), the workspace shape, the backlog flow algorithm (6 numbered steps), the YAML task template, goals-alignment rules, daily-guidance rules, categories, interaction style, and an 'Anticipate Next Actions' rule. CLAUDE.md exists as a thin Claude-specific pointer to the same instructions; core/templates/AGENTS.md is the seed copy that setup.sh installs.

GOALS.md (generated per-user by setup.sh) is the durable north-star context. Every task created from the backlog MUST cite a GOALS.md heading/bullet in its Context section; if no goal fits, the agent stops and asks whether to add a goal. Priority is capped structurally — P0 max 3, P1 max 7 (config.yaml: P0>3 / P1>5 warnings) — so relevance is enforced against goals, not just collected. Daily 'what should I work on' queries rank by priority+status+goal alignment, max 3 focus tasks.

Knowledge/ (git-ignored) holds briefs, research, specs, meeting notes. During backlog processing the agent searches Knowledge/ by keyword/project/date for context, and each task's YAML carries resource_refs: pointing at the relevant Knowledge/*.md files. Retrieval is plain markdown/file traversal + keyword search — explicitly NOT vector RAG (memory.md: 'no automatic deep retrieval system'; the agent only sees what is referenced or matches a search).

Pattern_raw_name: File-as-memory layered stack read at session start
How_implemented: memory.md frames the whole system as the fix for agents lacking persistent memory: a layered stack read every session — AGENTS.md (preferences) → GOALS.md (priorities) → Tasks/ (current state) → Knowledge/ (reference). Continuity across sessions is achieved by the durable Tasks/*.md files (status n/s/b/d + Progress Log) acting as a per-item journal rather than a single HANDOFF.md.

Pattern_raw_name: Per-task markdown files with YAML frontmatter + Progress Log
How_implemented: Each task is a standalone Tasks/<id>.md with YAML frontmatter (title, category, priority, status, created/due date, estimated_time, resource_refs) and body sections Context / Next Actions / Progress Log. Status is a one-letter state machine n→s→b→d; config.yaml ages tasks (flag inactive after 14 days, prune completed after 30 days) — a lightweight artifact lifecycle.

setup.sh creates the workspace directories (Tasks/, Knowledge/) and generates a personalized GOALS.md and the root AGENTS.md/BACKLOG.md from core/templates/, so a fresh clone becomes a populated context tree in one command.

BACKLOG.md is a raw capture inbox: the user brain-dumps unstructured notes; 'process backlog' extracts actionable items, dedups them, creates Tasks/ files, then the MCP clear_backlog tool resets BACKLOG.md to 'all done!'. A one-way capture funnel from inbox into structured durable context.

Root CLAUDE.md states the design philosophy and output-quality standards for the skill pack (frameworks over procedures, ALWAYS/NEVER/CRITICAL rules, startup-calibrated 3-50 person teams, no SAFe/RACI/velocity jargon, every skill must cite a real framework, 800-1200-word PRDs). It functions as an authoring-standards/governance doc for the repo rather than a runtime session-context loader — there is no tiered context tree, index, memory store, or session handoff machinery in this repo.

Root instruction file that sets the agent's identity ('pedagogic collaborator and skill extraction partner', 'ABC — Always Be Coaching'), defines the three skill types in a table, fixes the standard 6-part skill anatomy, and lays down non-negotiable output standards (Quality Checklist of 7 ticks: agent-ready, self-contained, opinionated, skimmable, zero-fluff; Tone Do/Don't; the load-bearing constraint 'Never strip explanation for brevity — coaching is the work'). Acts as an anti-drift governance doc for how every skill must be written.

Cross-harness root rules telling any agent to 'inspect available skills first before improvising', 'prefer the most specific skill', restrict to PM domains, and honor the pedagogic-first principle ('the PM should finish knowing more than when they started'). Mirrors CLAUDE.md for non-Claude runtimes (Codex/ChatGPT). Explicitly does NOT define subagent personas.

Pattern_raw_name: catalog/ generated indexes (skills-index.yaml, commands-index.yaml, skills-by-type.md, commands.md)
How_implemented: A scripts/generate-catalog.py build step scans every skill's frontmatter and emits two machine-readable indexes (skills-index.yaml with per-skill metadata, commands-index.yaml) plus two human-browse views (skills-by-type.md, commands.md). This is an explicit index/map of where each skill lives and what type/theme it is — retrieval via frontmatter scan, no vector DB. Frontmatter (name + description + best_for + scenarios) is the progressive-disclosure layer: an agent reads the index/descriptions to pick a skill, then drills into the full SKILL.md only when relevant.

Workflow skills and slash-commands are doc-as-baton pipelines: each phase produces an artifact consumed by the next (Phase 1 'problem hypothesis' feeds Phase 2 interview planning; outputs listed explicitly in the command frontmatter as 'Discovery plan / Prioritized assumptions / Validation experiment backlog'). Decision gates redirect flow ('Did experiments validate?' → pivot). Conflict rule: 'If there is a conflict, follow this file's workflow logic' over referenced skills.

SKILL.md is the only always-loaded file. It carries a 7-section intent→framework→'Load' routing table whose 'Load' column names exactly one knowledge file (e.g. knowledge/finance-metrics.md). At runtime the agent matches user intent, reads ONLY that one module, then drills to the section matching the Framework column ('Within the loaded module, find the section closest to the Framework column name'). The table itself is the index/map of where each framework lives — no vector DB, pure name-keyed retrieval. Knowledge stays out of context until a request needs it.

Pattern_raw_name: Two-tier quality gates (universal in SKILL.md + domain gates inside each module)
How_implemented: Tier-1 universal gates (assumptions labeled, outcomes measurable, roles specific, tradeoffs named, anti-patterns) live in SKILL.md and apply to every output. Tier-2 domain gates live INSIDE each knowledge module under a '## Quality Gates' section (e.g. finance's Vanity Metric Traps, Blended Metric Dangers, Calculation Errors) and only enter context when that module is loaded. Step 6 of the Execution Workflow forces checking both tiers.

SKILL.md '### Session Memory' instructs the agent to retain within-session facts (product stage, team structure, baseline metrics like MRR/churn/CAC, framework preferences, domain context) and reuse them, surfacing each recall with a visible, correctable tag: '[from earlier: Series A, 15-person team, $80k MRR]'. Explicitly scoped to one session — 'Do not assume context carries across separate sessions.' No persisted memory file.

The '## PM Sprint Workflow' table defines 6 ordered phases (Discover→Position→Prioritize→Specify→Validate→Measure), each with a 'Key Output' column (problem statement → positioning statement → RICE scores → PRD → validation plan → metrics dashboard). 'Each phase feeds output to the next' — the artifact of one phase is the input context to the next, with phase labels like '[Sprint: Phase 2/6 — Position]' and a back-pressure rule: if a later phase invalidates an earlier one, stop rather than build on a broken foundation.

ETHOS.md states three non-negotiable principles (Thinking Before Templating, Opinions With Tradeoffs, Compression Over Completeness) framed as anti-drift commitments — explicitly 'why it won't change to be more helpful in the generic sense.' Acts as the constitution the skill's behavior is justified against; the validator even greps README/SKILL for alignment phrases.

Single root instruction file defines identity ('personal productivity assistant'), workspace map, task/goal schema, the two core workflows (backlog-processing, daily-guidance), verification discipline, writing-style bans, and skills routing table. Per-runtime files are thin imports: CLAUDE.md is literally one line `@AGENTS.md`; CODEX.md/PI.md/OPENCLAW.md wrap the same source so all harnesses share one brain.

GOALS.md holds Current Goals (Why-it-matters + Key Results checkboxes + auto-populated Related-tasks), Ongoing focus areas, and a 'Not Now (Parked)' list with reasons, plus a weekly/monthly/quarterly review cadence. AGENTS.md hard-wires relevance to it: every task's Context section must reference a goal, the daily-guidance workflow cross-references GOALS.md before recommending max-3 focus items, and the agent is told to flag tasks that support no current goal.

BACKLOG.md is a quick-capture inbox; the backlog-processing workflow reads it, gathers Knowledge/ context, does a >60%-similarity dedup against existing Tasks/*.md, asks clarifying Qs for ambiguous items, materializes each confirmed item as a Tasks/<slug>.md file with YAML frontmatter, then clears BACKLOG.md back to a stub. Capture-loop + a lightweight lifecycle (backlog→task→status d→pruned after 30 days).

Pattern_raw_name: Task files with YAML frontmatter + resource_refs
How_implemented: Each Tasks/*.md carries frontmatter (title, category enum, priority P0-P3 with recommended caps, status n/s/b/d single-letter codes, dates, estimated_time, and a `resource_refs:` list pointing at Knowledge/*.md). Agent lists via glob `Tasks/*.md` then reads to parse frontmatter — IDs/paths in resource_refs are the lazy-retrieval handles to full Knowledge docs loaded on demand.

Per the README/skill standard, agents load only skill metadata (name, description, file path) up front and retrieve the full SKILL.md body only when a skill is selected, to preserve context budget over long runs. Reinforced by each SKILL.md's frontmatter being a tight name+description pair and each skill carrying a `When to Use`/`When Not to Use` gate for selection.

ost-intake is an explicit input-normalization stage that collects/cleans four required fields (business_outcome, journey_nodes_as_list as JSON array, interview transcripts, constraints) and emits 'Final normalized values for all four required fields' as the artifact the downstream opportunity-solution-tree skill consumes — a doc-as-baton between two skills.

After significant sessions the agent writes Evals/YYYY-MM-DD-*.md from a fixed template (Judgement enum success/partial/failure/pending, axial-code checkboxes like good-context-gathering / over-engineering / missed-requirements, plus an explicit 'AGENTS.md Improvements' field). A weekly maintenance task reviews recent evals and folds learnings back into AGENTS.md — a self-correction loop that rewrites the governing instruction doc.

Pattern_raw_name: Privacy split: gitignored user data vs committed config
How_implemented: Tasks/, Knowledge/, BACKLOG.md, Resources/ are local/gitignored; only AGENTS.md/GOALS.md/skills/System are version-controlled — a deliberate context-vs-private-data boundary (does not map cleanly to a CM code).

Pattern_raw_name: CLAUDE.md (single source of truth) + AGENTS.md redirect
How_implemented: Root CLAUDE.md holds repo identity, the skills=nouns/commands=verbs design rule, frontmatter contracts, version-sync rules, and the 'Further Reading neutral tone' output standard. AGENTS.md is a deliberate one-line redirect ('all agent guidance lives in CLAUDE.md — do not duplicate here') so non-Claude harnesses that look for AGENTS.md get pointed to the single source rather than a forked copy.

CLAUDE.md mandates 'keep frontmatter lean; detailed content goes in body (progressive disclosure)'. Skills auto-load only their name+description into context until contextually relevant (description-match trigger); the full method body is read on demand. No tiered index/map file — relevance is the trigger, not an index.

Slash commands run skills in a fixed order where each step's markdown output (ideas → assumptions matrix → prioritized assumptions → experiments → discovery plan) feeds the next; ship-check chains document-app → security-audit-static → performance-audit-static → derive-tests, each consuming the prior doc. The chaining is prompt-level orchestration in a single command body, not a persisted artifact store — the 'baton' lives only in the running conversation.

The pm-ai-shipping plugin writes a fixed set of starter docs for an AI-built app (architecture, flows, permissions, variables/secrets, test-coverage; conditional docs for emails/cron) BEFORE auditing. These generated docs become the source-of-truth spec the later audit/test commands execute against — i.e. a command scaffolds N context files into a fixed doc set, then downstream skills read them.

Weak document-handoff at best: each skill's SKILL.md links to a single references/example.md worked output. There is no cross-skill artifact chaining, no index/map file, no CLAUDE.md root instruction file, no memory/KB, no handoff journal. The repo deliberately has near-zero context-management machinery — it is a pure stateless skill catalog (root contains only LICENSE, README.md, skills/).

Root-level AGENTS.md is the cross-harness identity+routing doc: it catalogs all skills by phase classification, documents output-standard contracts (Meeting Skills Contract, Foundation/Design Sprint Contracts), lists routing rules/refusal guardrails, and names the sub-agent roster. Acts as the index/map a non-Claude agent reads to discover what skills exist and how to route. Paired with CLAUDE.md which carries house-style rules (no em-dash) and public-vs-private documentation rules.

Discovery is frontmatter-driven: an internal pm-skill-router sub-agent (not user-facing) matches an incoming query against each skill's description: field to pick the single best skill — a 'key-free engine' routing on semantic intent rather than command patterns. Skill bodies are only drilled into once the description matches, so the catalog stays as lightweight progressive-disclosure summaries until a skill is selected.

Pattern_raw_name: phase-router.mjs SessionStart hook (phase detection → skill injection)
How_implemented: A SessionStart hook detects the current Triple-Diamond phase from two signals — git branch name (branchPhase) and working-dir artifact structure (artifactPhase) — reconciles them via resolvePhase(), then looks up the top-4 skills for that phase from a phase-map built off SKILL.md classifications and injects a one-line message into session context: 'You appear to be in the [Phase] phase... Relevant pm-skills: [...]. Invoke the one that fits.' Stays silent (returns null) on any failure. This is lifecycle-hook-driven, goal/phase-anchored context priming.

A PreToolUse hook scans outgoing tool calls for banned characters/patterns (em-dash/en-dash, placeholders, fabricated metrics) and blocks them before they're written, enforcing the CLAUDE.md house rules at the harness level rather than relying on the model to remember. Registered via hooks.json with matchers; lib/local-config.mjs lets a repo-local .claude/pm-skills.local.md tune behavior.

A reserved underscore-prefixed dir holding cross-session/cross-agent continuity: DECISIONS.md (durable decision log), session-log/ (interaction records), and claude/ + codex/ subdirs for per-harness context. Functions as a session-handoff/journal layer preserving operational continuity across agent instances and harnesses.

Each workflow file (e.g. feature-kickoff.md) defines an ordered skill chain where each step's artifact explicitly feeds the next ('Problem → Hypothesis → PRD → User Stories → Launch'), with named handoff guidance describing what context each artifact passes downstream. The PRD/problem-statement artifacts are the durable source-of-truth that later steps execute against.

For multi-step runs the pm-workflow-orchestrator persists each confirmed step artifact to disk at _pm-skills/plan-orchestrator/<run>/; downstream steps read the confirmed on-disk version. On a user edit at a checkpoint, both the in-context capture and the disk file are overwritten atomically before advancing — keeping context and disk artifact in sync as the baton passes.

A single shared markdown file (.agents/startup-context.md) holds 8 fixed sections — Company, Product, Market, Business Model, Team, Fundraising, Goals — that every other skill is contractually required to `reads: [startup-context]` and 'read this context first' before generating output, so all 50 skills produce company-tailored rather than generic output. If the file is missing, the startup-context skill interviews the founder across three rounds and writes the file. Data philosophy: factual-only, unknown fields marked 'TBD' rather than speculated. Acts as the always-loaded core-facts preamble AND the context-scaffolding setup step (it writes the one canonical context file the rest of the system reads).

Each SKILL.md frontmatter carries `reads: [startup-context]` (declares its context dependency) and `related: [skillA, skillB]` (declares its neighbors in the workflow graph, e.g. prd-writing → roadmap-planning, mvp-scoping). This is a lightweight declarative index of where context lives and which skills chain — no vector DB, no runtime retrieval engine; the agent follows the `reads`/`related` pointers as a static map. Frontmatter `description`/`trigger` ('When the user needs to...') is the progressive-disclosure layer the agent scans before drilling into the body.

prd-writing produces a durable 8-section PRD (Summary, Contacts, Background, Objective, Market Segment(s), Value Proposition(s), Solution, Release) that downstream skills (roadmap-planning, mvp-scoping) consume via the `related` chain — the written spec is the artifact the next step reads.

Pattern_raw_name: Stateless phase pipeline anchored on .atlas-ai/state/pipeline.json (go orchestrator + current_phase())
How_implemented: The `go` orchestrator holds NO local state. Every phase skill calls mcp__plugin_prd_go__current_phase() on entry to read pipeline.json, so the pipeline 'survives context loss' — a compaction or fresh session re-derives where it is. Routing is a pure switch: null/SETUP→/prd:setup, DISCOVER→/prd:discover, GENERATE→/prd:generate, HANDOFF→/prd:handoff, EXECUTE→/prd:execute-task; it re-checks phase after each skill returns. The state file plus per-phase artifacts (prd.md, tasks.json) are the durable continuity layer across sessions.

.taskmaster/docs/prd.md is the graded durable spec; it is parsed into .taskmaster/tasks/tasks.json (dependencies, complexity scores, subtasks, research_notes). AGENTS.md moat #3 is 'persistent vendor-neutral task graph' — tasks.json is 'plain repo files [that] survive vendor swaps' across Claude/Codex/Gemini and across sessions. Tasks store IDs + research notes and are loaded just-in-time by execution; workers receive task JSON embedded in their prompt rather than reading shared files.

Pattern_raw_name: CLAUDE.md (Atlas north-star) + AGENTS.md (harness-neutral operating contract) + SKILL.md (normative spec)
How_implemented: Three-tier instruction split with explicit roles: 'SKILL.md = normative behavior spec; AGENTS.md = harness-neutral operating contract; CLAUDE.md = north-star/identity.' CLAUDE.md encodes non-negotiable invariants ('four moats', 'unfakable Done = oracle-PASS AND code WIRED/EXEMPT', stdlib-only Python, flock-guarded telemetry appends, fail-closed gates) that act as an anti-drift constitution; 'documentation must align with implementation, not vice versa' and structural tests enforce these doc invariants in CI.

Pattern_raw_name: CONSTRAINTS CAPTURED block carried from discovery into the spec
How_implemented: discover must emit a typed 'CONSTRAINTS CAPTURED:' block (tech stack, timeline, team, budget, integrations, regulatory, domain restrictions) and pass it forward as advance_phase evidence. generate's Step 3 then enforces that EVERY captured constraint reappears in prd.md or the phase halts — 'if a constraint is mentioned in discovery but missing from the spec, that's a bug.' A typed payload is the baton handed between phases, gate-checked at the seam.

Pattern_raw_name: Durable worker inbox + structured execute-log.jsonl + feedback.jsonl
How_implemented: Fleet workers report results through a 'durable inbox rather than agent claims' using exactly four terminal statuses (DONE / DONE_WITH_CONCERNS / NEEDS_CONTEXT / BLOCKED) — the orchestrator trusts the inbox + CDD evidence cards, not the worker's prose, a context-firewall against unverified narrative. Every execute iteration appends strictly-typed JSON to .atlas-ai/state/execute-log.jsonl (narrative text in a boolean field is a logging violation); feedback_submit appends run outcomes to feedback.jsonl. A Stop hook (evidence_gate.py) and SessionStart-style heartbeat cron persist/guard state across the lifecycle.

Pattern_raw_name: Worker-embedded task JSON with sole-writer control plane
How_implemented: Each fleet worker gets a fresh isolated context: a dedicated git worktree + branch and the FULL task JSON embedded directly in its prompt, so it never reads the shared tasks.json. The 'Sole-Writer Rule' makes ONLY the orchestrator mutate tasks.json and pipeline.json — eliminating control-plane races and keeping each worker a clean, one-task-one-context unit.

Pattern_raw_name: PRD/Epic/Task files in .claude/ as source of truth ('Requirements live in files, not heads')
How_implemented: Every artifact is a markdown file with YAML frontmatter under a fixed tree: .claude/prds/<name>.md (PRD) -> .claude/epics/<name>/epic.md (technical epic, links back to PRD via metadata) -> .claude/epics/<name>/NNN.md (numbered task files) -> renamed to GitHub issue numbers after sync.
