Each phase reads the prior phase's file and writes the next (doc-as-baton): plan reads PRD->writes epic, structure reads epic->writes tasks, execute reads task+analysis->writes code+progress. State persists across sessions in files, not chat, so 'context evaporation' between sessions is avoided.

Pattern_raw_name: GitHub Issues as the coordination database
How_implemented: After sync.md runs, the canonical task store moves OUT of local files into GitHub: `gh issue create` makes one issue per epic/task, the optional yahsan2/gh-sub-issue extension creates a parent epic issue with child sub-issues for true hierarchy, and local NNN.md files are renamed to the returned issue numbers (sed rewrites depends_on/conflicts_with arrays to real issue numbers, frontmatter gets the github: URL). Agents then coordinate via issue comments + commits referencing 'Issue #N', so progress lives in a shared external DB humans and other tools can see — not in any single agent's window.

SKILL.md is the always-loaded Tier-1 router (just frontmatter + an intent->reference table). It names one reference file per lifecycle phase (plan/structure/sync/execute/track) plus a shared conventions.md; the model loads only the reference matching the detected intent (progressive disclosure). Reference files in turn point to deterministic bash scripts for any read-only query, so heavy procedural detail and constants stay out of context until the phase is active.

Each parallel agent writes its own progress file at .claude/epics/<epic>/updates/<N>/stream-<X>.md (frontmatter: issue, stream, started, status) and is restricted to an assigned file scope from the analysis file. This is the repo's real 'context firewall': agents don't dump raw work into the main thread — they isolate by file scope and surface state through per-stream files + commit messages, so the orchestrator sees structured status rather than each agent's full reasoning. NOTE: I found NO explicit 'sub-agent returns a compact summary' instruction; the firewall here is scope+file isolation, not an enforced summary-return contract.

track.md states tracking 'use[s] bash scripts directly for speed and consistency — the LLM is not needed; just run the script and present the output.' 14 scripts (status.sh, standup.sh, blocked.sh, next.sh, epic-status.sh, validate.sh, search.sh, etc.) scan the .claude/epics/ file tree and emit fresh status, so the model retrieves state on demand via grep-like scripts instead of holding it in context or using a vector store.

Pattern_raw_name: .claude/context/ shared project context + epic archival lifecycle
How_implemented: conventions.md defines a .claude/context/ dir for shared project context and .claude/epics/archived/ for completed epics — epics move from active -> archived as a lifecycle/state-machine on the folder, and progress is recalculated by counting closed tasks against total when tasks close.

A single declarative config.yaml holds always-loaded core facts (user_name, project_name, communication_language, document_output_language, output_folder, user_skill_level beginner/intermediate/expert) plus path indices (planning_artifacts, implementation_artifacts, project_knowledge=docs) and the 6-member agent roster (Mary/analyst, Paige, John/PM, Sally/UX, Winston/architect, Amelia/dev with codes+icons). Every agent step-4 'loads persistent facts and config' from it; it doubles as the map of where artifacts live (no vector DB).

Root AGENTS.md sets cross-harness identity + non-negotiable rules: Conventional Commits for every commit; pre-push 'npm ci && npm run quality' replicating .github/workflows/quality.yaml; skill validation via 'npm run validate:skills'. It is the framework's contributor-facing root instruction file rather than a per-project context root.

Pattern_raw_name: planning-artifacts → implementation-artifacts doc-baton chain with SELECTIVE_LOAD
How_implemented: Each SDLC phase writes a markdown artifact the next phase reads. bmad-create-story step-2/3 SELECTIVE_LOADs epics/PRD/architecture/UX from {planning_artifacts}/*epic*.md etc. (whole OR sharded), reads sprint-status.yaml and prior story files, then emits {implementation_artifacts}/{{story_key}}.md (e.g. 1-2-user-authentication.md) with a fat 'Dev Notes / References' section citing source doc paths+sections — the story file is the durable baton the dev agent executes against. SELECTIVE_LOAD = lazy: pull only the relevant shard, not the whole doc.

Splits a large markdown doc (e.g. architecture.md) into one file per level-2 (##) section under a folder + an index.md navigation hub, via 'npx @kayvan/markdown-tree-parser explode'. Produces a Tier-2 index + Tier-3 just-in-time section files so downstream skills load single shards rather than the monolith. Explicitly instructs deleting/archiving the original to avoid duplicate-source confusion; shards are concatenable to reconstruct.

Distills any messy input (brain dump, PRD, transcript, email) into a SPEC.md kernel of 5 fields (Why, Capabilities-with-stable-IDs, Constraints, Non-goals, Success-signal) inside a folder. The authority is .memlog.md, an APPEND-ONLY chronological log; SPEC.md is RE-DERIVED from the memlog every run (never hand-edited), so multiple upstream skills (PRD/UX/arch) can feed updates in any order with no merge drift, and stable capability IDs survive across updates. A companions: array lets consumers discover all referenced files. 'Every downstream BMad skill consumes' this contract.

Each agent's customize.toml declares persistent_facts that resolve file globs (PM loads file:{project-root}/**/project-context.md) and inject them as always-present session facts. Combined with activation_steps_prepend/append hooks, this scaffolds a fixed per-project context surface every persona reloads on activation.
Core skill that builds/maintains an index of project docs (map of where knowledge lives) for retrieval — pairs with shard-doc's index.md hubs to give file-traversal retrieval without a vector store.

Standards are documented codebase conventions stored as agent-os/standards/[folder]/[standard].md (folders like api/, database/, javascript/, css/, testing/; root = files directly under standards/). A machine-readable map lives at agent-os/standards/index.yml in the shape `folder: { file: { description: '...' } }`, alphabetized by folder then file, .md extensions stripped, single-line descriptions — a pure markdown/YAML index with NO vector DB. /inject-standards reads ONLY the index first (cheap), analyzes the current conversation, then loads the 2-5 full standard files that actually apply just-in-time. This is explicit progressive disclosure: index acts as the Tier-2 'map of where knowledge lives', full standard bodies are Tier-3 loaded on demand.

Pattern_raw_name: shape-spec timestamped spec folder (plan.md / shape.md / standards.md / references.md / visuals/)
How_implemented: The /shape-spec command (must run in Plan mode) produces a per-feature folder named `[timestamp]-[feature-slug]` containing five durable artifacts: plan.md (the executable plan), shape.md (decisions + context/rationale), standards.md (the exact technical guidelines that apply, copied in so the spec is self-contained), references.md (pointers to similar existing code to imitate), and visuals/ (mockups/screenshots). Hard rule: Task 1 of every plan is ALWAYS 'Save spec documentation' BEFORE any implementation — so the written spec is persisted and discoverable as the source of truth before code is touched. Each spec folder is a self-contained change unit (folder-per-change) and a doc-handoff baton: shape → plan → implementation reads forward.

/plan-product writes three starter product-context files into a fixed folder agent-os/product/: mission.md (problem, target users, unique solution), roadmap.md (phased MVP + post-launch features), tech-stack.md (frontend/backend/db/infra choices). /shape-spec step 4 ('Check product goals') reads these when planning a feature, so product mission/roadmap anchor relevance of new specs. tech-stack.md doubles as a seed standard shipped in profiles/default/global/.

Pattern_raw_name: profiles + config.yml inheritance (base install vs project install)
How_implemented: config.yml (version: 3.0, default_profile: default) defines named profiles with optional `inherits_from` chains. A base install holds reusable standards under profiles/<name>/ (e.g. profiles/default/global/tech-stack.md). scripts/project-install.sh resolves the active profile + inheritance chain and copies that profile's standards into PROJECT_DIR/agent-os/standards/, then auto-generates standards/index.yml, then copies the 5 commands into .claude/commands/agent-os/. sync-to-profile.sh pushes project-refined standards back up to the shared profile — a two-way standards library across projects.

Root CLAUDE.md sets plugin identity and the hard operating rule that in Cowork (hosted VM) there is no GitHub API, so codebase analysis MUST use local Glob/Grep/Read against shared directories; users must share folders before analysis. Defines the 3-command workflow surface.

Pattern_raw_name: Skill split into 6 just-in-time reference files
How_implemented: SKILL.md is the always-loaded core; it defers full specs to sibling files loaded on demand: workflow-phases.md, output-formats.md, enterprise-requirements.md, engines-and-innovations.md, rag-and-reference.md, plus skill-config.json. SKILL.md holds the index/pointers ('detailed in external file engines-and-innovations.md') so heavy spec text is pulled only when the relevant phase runs.

/index-codebase scans a repo (respecting .gitignore) and extracts architectural patterns (Repository/Service/Factory/Observer/Strategy/MVVM/Clean Arch), domain entities, and dependency relationships into a retained context keyed by a codebaseId. Retrieval is Contextual BM25: LLM-generated context prepended to chunks, hybrid 70% vector + 30% BM25 fused via Reciprocal Rank Fusion (k=60), 5-10 (cap 25) chunks. PRD-type sets 'RAG hop' depth (1-4) for multi-step dependency traversal. Stored in the Swift library's Database layer.

Generation writes 9 sequential markdown artifacts (prd-overview → requirements → user-stories → technical → acceptance → roadmap → jira → tests → verification) via the Write tool, each section written immediately and used as the durable spec the next section/JIRA/test step traces against. FR traceability (Rule 10) forces every requirement to cite its source artifact.

Pattern_raw_name: PRD-type context table (per-type sections / Qs / RAG hops / strategy)
How_implemented: A static table in SKILL.md maps each of 8 PRD types to its section count, clarification-question range, RAG hop depth, and strategy focus — a deterministic 'map' that drives how much context to load and how deep to search per document type, exposed at runtime via the MCP tool get_prd_context_info.

The durable input is a written PRD file at .taskmaster/docs/prd.txt. `parse-prd` runs an Analysis→Generation→Enhancement pass (extract requirements, identify components, detect dependencies, estimate complexity → generate 10-15 sequenced tasks with acceptance criteria & test strategy). The PRD is the baton: it is parsed once into tasks.json, and every downstream command/agent executes against the generated tasks rather than re-reading the PRD — a doc-handoff from spec to structured work items.

Pattern_raw_name: tasks.json single-file store with tag partitioning (pluggable storage adapter)
How_implemented: All task state persists outside the LLM window in ONE .taskmaster/tasks/tasks.json file. A FileStorage class implements an IStorage interface, decomposed into FormatHandler (serialization + tag extraction), FileOperations (IO), and PathResolver. Tasks are partitioned by TAG (TaskTag = name + task-id list + metadata) so multiple workstreams live in one file. A StorageType union ('file' | 'api' | 'auto') swaps the same interface for a Supabase-backed remote store, so agents read/write task objects by id without the store living in context. The task list itself is the goal-anchored state that `next-task` and the orchestrator prioritize against.

Pattern_raw_name: Task as rich context object (TaskImplementationMetadata + user metadata)
How_implemented: The Task interface (packages/tm-core/src/common/types/index.ts) is not a thin id/title record — it carries the full execution context for handoff: details, testStrategy, dependencies[], plus an extended TaskImplementationMetadata block (relevantFiles, codebasePatterns, existingInfrastructure, scopeBoundaries, implementationApproach, technicalConstraints, acceptanceCriteria, skills, category) and a user-defined `metadata` field explicitly designed to survive all task operations. The orchestrator hands an executor a task id and the executor pulls this whole object — the task object IS the typed context packet passed between agents.

Pattern_raw_name: Subtask update log (logging decisions into the task)
How_implemented: The task-executor logs implementation decisions back into the task via `update-subtask`, and marks status in-progress→done via `set-status`. Each subtask thus accrues a running record of what was tried/decided, which the checker and any fresh executor later read — a per-task capture loop that keeps learnings attached to the work item rather than in chat scrollback.

Each proposed change is a self-contained folder under openspec/changes/<kebab-name>/ holding proposal.md, specs/<capability>/spec.md, design.md, tasks.md. On completion the folder moves to openspec/changes/archive/<YYYY-MM-DD>-<name>/ (date-prefixed) and the deltas are merged into the durable openspec/specs/ source-of-truth tree. Each artifact is the baton the next step reads (proposal -> specs/design -> tasks -> apply), so the folder IS the change's lifecycle state.

Pattern_raw_name: schema.yaml artifact graph + requires[] dependency gating
How_implemented: schemas/spec-driven/schema.yaml declares ordered artifacts each with id, generates (output glob e.g. proposal.md, specs/**/*.md), requires (e.g. tasks requires [specs, design]; specs/design require [proposal]) and an embedded multi-paragraph instructions string. src/core/artifact-graph/{graph,resolver,state}.ts build a DAG; state.ts detectCompleted() infers progress purely from file existence on disk (artifactOutputExists with glob support); resolver gates each artifact behind its requires set. This declarative DAG governs the whole propose->apply machine — a constitution-like contract enforced in code, not just prose.

Pattern_raw_name: openspec specs/ source-of-truth tree + delta spec operations
How_implemented: openspec/specs/<capability>/spec.md holds the durable, current behavior of the system. A change never edits these directly; it writes DELTA spec files under its own changes/.../specs/ using operation headers (## ADDED / MODIFIED / REMOVED / RENAMED Requirements). MODIFIED requires copying the ENTIRE existing requirement block and editing it. On archive, deltas are reconciled into the canonical specs/ tree — a delta-aware, self-evolving spec store.

Pattern_raw_name: CLI as just-in-time instruction server (openspec instructions/status --json)
How_implemented: Rather than dumping all rules into the agent's context, the slash-command prompt tells the agent to SHELL OUT to the CLI at runtime: `openspec instructions <artifact-id> --change <name> --json` returns the template + rules + output path for ONLY the artifact being built right now, and `openspec status --json` returns completed/pending artifacts + applyRequires so the agent knows the next step. Context is pulled lazily per-step from the schema rather than preloaded.

openspec init scaffolds the openspec/ folder and writes root agent-instruction files (AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md per harness) plus a project.md/config that teach the AI assistant the propose/apply/archive workflow and where artifacts live; `openspec update` refreshes these. This is the always-loaded root identity + a context-scaffolding setup step.

Pattern_raw_name: specs/<feature-id>/ artifact chain (spec.md → research.md → data-model.md → contracts/ → quickstart.md → plan.md → tasks.md)
How_implemented: Each command writes a file the next consumes. /specify creates specs/<NNN-or-timestamp-shortname>/spec.md from the resolved spec-template; /plan reads spec.md + constitution, emits research.md (Phase 0), then data-model.md/contracts/quickstart.md (Phase 1); /tasks reads plan.md+spec.md+optional design docs to emit tasks.md; /implement reads tasks.md+plan.md. The spec is the durable source of truth — 'specifications become executable, directly generating working implementations.' The resolved feature dir is persisted to .specify/feature.json so downstream commands locate it without relying on git branch naming.

A versioned (semver: MAJOR/MINOR/PATCH) governance doc of non-negotiable principles. /constitution fills a placeholder template ([PRINCIPLE_1_NAME] etc.), increments the version, prepends an HTML-comment 'Sync Impact Report', and propagates amendments into plan/spec/tasks templates. Every downstream command does 'IF EXISTS: Load /memory/constitution.md'. /analyze treats constitution conflicts as automatically CRITICAL and forbids 'dilution, reinterpretation, or silent ignoring' — anti-drift gate.

Pattern_raw_name: per-agent context file (CLAUDE.md / GEMINI.md / etc.) + agent-context extension
How_implemented: Each integration declares context_file = 'CLAUDE.md' (or GEMINI.md, AGENTS.md...). The bundled agent-context extension (config at .specify/extensions/agent-context/agent-context-config.yml) manages a marked Spec Kit section inside the agent's root context file; plan.md Phase 1 includes 'Update agent context by running the agent script.' base.py resolves context_files from the extension config (supporting multiple files) and falls back to the integration's declared default, replacing the old per-agent wrapper scripts.

/analyze explicitly says 'Load Artifacts (Progressive Disclosure): Load only the minimal necessary context from each artifact' — pulling just Overview/FRs/Success Criteria from spec, just architecture/phases from plan, just task IDs/[P] markers from tasks — and builds internal semantic models WITHOUT echoing raw artifacts into output. Findings capped at 50 to stay token-efficient.

feature.json stores only the resolved feature_directory path (an ID/key), letting later commands lazily re-locate the working set instead of re-deriving it. init-options.json holds feature_numbering strategy ('sequential' NNN vs 'timestamp' YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS) read at runtime.

Pattern_raw_name: context_packet JSON (in-memory, threaded through all phases)
How_implemented: prd-new.md PHASE 0 initializes a single JSON object with fixed slots (identity, business, features, regulatory, integrations, infrastructure{vcs,branch_strategy}, stack, repo_structure='monorepo', research_refs[], pending_research[]). Each interrogation block writes into its slot; every downstream Agent dispatch passes a typed slice of this packet (e.g. requirements-analyst gets {context_packet, validated_research}; skills-generator gets {context_packet, prd_version}) — README states agents receive structured data 'never as prose, always structured'. research_refs carry only ref_id/topic/findings/sources, i.e. IDs+summaries rather than full pages.

prd-new PHASE 2 dispatches research-validator as a fresh Agent call 'no context reuse', passing ONLY the research_refs. The agent prompt opens with 'You have NO prior context from the session... This isolation is what makes validation meaningful' — the heavy research output is audited in a clean window and only a compact approved/rejected verdict (ref_results + summary JSON) returns to the orchestrator. Anti confirmation-bias by construction.

Fixed phase order where each phase's structured output is the next phase's input: interrogation packet -> official-researcher findings -> requirements-analyst RF/RNF/DR/compliance -> architecture-designer stack+ADRs -> prd-writer emits PRD.md/ARCHITECTURE.md/ER.md. PRD.md is then the durable source of truth that all 7 generated skills cite (RF-/DR-/ADR- IDs).

Pattern_raw_name: docs/prd/PRD.md as versioned source of truth + prd_version sync gate
How_implemented: PRD.md carries a Version: X.Y header; every generated skill carries a matching prd_version in YAML frontmatter. prd-guardian compares skill prd_version vs PRD header and warns 'Project skills are stale (skill v1.2, PRD v1.3). Run /prd-evolve to sync' — a drift gate. project-guardian additionally enforces non-negotiable Hard Blocks (no separate-repo service, no off-stack tech, no PII without encryption, no DR violation) functioning as a constitution.

prd-evolve reads current PRD/ARCHITECTURE/skills, runs requirements-analyst in mode:'delta' which returns delta_type + affected_artifacts + new_prd_version, then dispatches ONLY the impacted agents in parallel and updates ONLY listed sections (README impact table maps change-type -> artifacts, e.g. 'new domain rule' -> PRD.md §4 + project-domain-rules + project-guardian). Skills bump prd_version/last_evolved and append an Evolution Log row rather than being regenerated. Unaffected artifacts are explicitly preserved and reported.

Pattern_raw_name: docs/stack/ local documentation cache + PreToolUse hook
How_implemented: stack-guide-generator writes .claude/settings.json with a PreToolUse hook matching 'WebFetch|WebSearch|mcp__context7__query-docs' whose command echoes a stderr reminder to check docs/stack/ first and save fetched docs after. docs/stack/README.md is a table index (File|Technology|Topic|Version|Date); cached files are {technology}-{topic}.md with a Source/Retrieved/Version header. project-docs-stack skill encodes the local-first protocol with a Hard Rule: 'Never perform two web searches for the same technology/topic in the same project lifetime.' A file-on-disk persistent doc memory across sessions, no vector DB.

Pattern_raw_name: Tiered root + index + living docs tree (CLAUDE.md / docs/index.md / sub-files)
How_implemented: Generates root + per-layer CLAUDE.md (backend/frontend/infrastructure) as always-loaded rules; docs/index.md is the Tier-2 map linking 6 sub-files (business/architecture/structure/development/tech-stack/local-setup) generated just-in-time from PRD+ARCHITECTURE+source tree. project-docs skill maintains this as living documentation with CREATE vs UPDATE modes (mode detected by presence of docs/index.md): UPDATE diffs docs vs current implementation, keeps/updates/deletes sections, adds 'Verify' callouts when unsure.

official-researcher output nests sources {url,title,domain,retrieved,is_official} under each finding with key_specs that must be 'directly verifiable at the cited URL'; research-validator spot-fetches 1-2 URLs/ref to confirm reachability and factual match before approval. Source-linked, confidence-graded (approved/partially_approved/rejected).

Pattern_raw_name: `.apm/` structured state tree (spec.md / plan.md / tracker.md / memory/index.md) — 'project state lives in structured files outside any agent's context'
How_implemented: `apm init` scaffolds a fixed `.apm/` folder. The Planner populates spec.md (requirements/source-of-truth), plan.md (work breakdown into Stages/Tasks), tracker.md (live task/worker/version-control tables), and memory/index.md (an index of where memory logs live + stage summaries). Agents read these durable files rather than holding state in-context; the Spec is the executable source of truth the whole workflow runs against. tracker.md drives prioritization (which Stage/Task is active).

memory/index.md is a lightweight INDEX (memory notes + condensed stage summaries) pointing at the granular Task Logs, which are stored just-in-time under `memory/stage-<NN>/`. Workers append outcomes to a pre-constructed `log_path` (handed to them in their Task Prompt frontmatter); the Manager reads current-stage logs by default and pulls previous-stage logs only on demand when dependency context emerges — explicit lazy retrieval keyed off the index rather than loading all history.

Each Worker (and the Manager) gets a bus directory with three single-purpose channel files. The Manager writes a self-contained Task Prompt to `task.md`; the Worker reads it, executes, writes a Task Report to `report.md`; handoff prompts go to `handoff.md`. Protocol rule: 'always read a bus file before writing to it' (clears the channel, ensures platform recognition of the change). Agent identity resolves from the directory slug (Frontend Agent → frontend-agent). This is a typed, doc-as-baton handoff between agents using the filesystem as the transport — a state machine of bus files rather than in-context message passing.

Pattern_raw_name: Handoff Log + Handoff Prompt (session-handoff to fresh agent)
How_implemented: When an agent's context fills, a handoff command writes TWO artifacts that deliberately separate PAST from PRESENT: (1) a Handoff Log at `.apm/memory/handoffs/manager/handoff-<NN>.log.md` (YAML frontmatter: agent type, instance numbers, handoff sequence, stage) capturing supplementary working knowledge NOT in the planning docs/logs — coordination overview, tracked Worker-handoff table, version-control state (branches/worktrees/pending merges), learned User preferences, and coordination insights; and (2) a Handoff Prompt on the handoff bus giving the incoming instance outstanding tasks + current-stage progress/blockers + reconstruction procedure. The fresh agent reads the log, reads current-stage Task Logs, and emits a state summary before acting.

Pattern_raw_name: Session summarize + Archive + Archive Explorer recall
How_implemented: `/apm-8-summarize-session` produces a session summary; completed sessions land in `.apm/archives/`. On a NEW session the Planner's context-gathering phase checks for `.apm/archives/` and spawns the apm-archive-explorer subagent, which reads summaries-first then spec/plan, synthesizes into five categories, and returns file-path verification handles so the Planner can spot-check stale context against the live codebase — a capture-and-recall loop across whole projects, not just sessions.

Pattern_raw_name: Rules file at workspace root + workflow-spec top-down propagation
How_implemented: The Planner writes a Rules file (`{RULES_FILE}`) at the repo ROOT (the always-loaded root instruction doc — e.g. AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md-style) holding execution guidelines all agents inherit. The customization skill enforces a governance rule: 'Any change that affects APM's behavior, procedures, or coordination patterns must be reflected in the workflow specification BEFORE modifying runtime files' — a constitution/spec-first anti-drift gate under `templates/_standards/`.

create-prd.md instructs the assistant to NOT implement, instead to write a structured PRD (Introduction, Goals, User Stories, Functional Requirements [numbered], Non-Goals, Design/Technical Considerations, Success Metrics, Open Questions) and SAVE it to /tasks/prd-[feature-name].md. This file becomes the durable spec the next stage reads via @MyFeature-PRD.md. The PRD is the artifact that carries intent across the prompt boundary — context lives in a file on disk, not in the chat window.

Pattern_raw_name: Document-handoff chain (PRD → tasks file → checkbox execution)
How_implemented: Three-stage doc-as-baton: (1) create-prd.md writes prd-[feature-name].md; (2) generate-tasks.md reads that PRD (@prd-file + @generate-tasks.md) and writes tasks-[feature-name].md containing a Relevant Files list + hierarchical checkbox tasks; (3) execution reads the tasks file and mutates it in place (`- [ ]`→`- [x]`). Each step's output file is the literal input to the next; the tasks file doubles as both plan and live progress ledger.

Pattern_raw_name: Tasks file as in-place progress state (`- [ ]`→`- [x]` after every sub-task)
How_implemented: generate-tasks.md embeds an 'Instructions for Completing Tasks' section: the assistant must check off each item in the markdown file by editing `- [ ]` to `- [x]` AFTER EACH SUB-TASK (not per parent task). The tasks-*.md file is therefore a goal-anchored, self-updating state machine of remaining work that survives context loss — re-opening the file tells any fresh session exactly where execution stands.

Pattern_raw_name: Relevant Files registry inside the task list
How_implemented: generate-tasks.md mandates a 'Relevant Files' section listing each code file to create/modify plus its paired test file, each with a one-line rationale. This is a lightweight, file-level map of where the change lives (no vector DB) — an index the implementing agent consults to keep edits scoped.

Pattern_raw_name: CLAUDE.md as lean config (not manual), <60 lines, [FILL IN] placeholders
How_implemented: Single project-root CLAUDE.md auto-loaded at session start. Top block is a 9-field '# PM Context' fill-in form (Role/Company/Product/Target users/Current focus/Primary metric/Guardrails/OKRs/Terminology) — a tiny always-loaded core-facts preamble the user customizes in ~2 min. Followed by Writing Rules (banned-words list: delve/landscape/synergy/leverage/robust/streamline/cutting-edge; lead-with-recommendation; never fabricate, use `[NEED: data from X]`). Design thesis stated explicitly: 'intentionally under 60 lines; Claude follows short, specific instructions better than long ones'; troubleshooting warns 'beyond ~150 lines Claude starts ignoring instructions — prune aggressively, move domain knowledge into skills.' The [FILL IN] scaffolding is a manual, human-filled variant of context-scaffolding rather than a command that writes files.

A code block in CLAUDE.md documents an intended tiered layout: `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` (personal defaults, all projects) -> `./CLAUDE.md` (project-level, git-shared) -> `.claude/rules/*.md` (modular rules scoped by glob) -> `.claude/skills/*/SKILL.md` (task workflows, loaded on demand). Routing rule: 'Domain knowledge -> skills. Scoped rules -> .claude/rules/. Universal behavior -> this file.' This is a map/index of where knowledge lives, but partly aspirational — `.claude/rules/` ships empty/absent in the repo (404), so the tiering is documented intent more than working tree.

Pattern_raw_name: Context Management section + @-references + Plan Mode rules
How_implemented: CLAUDE.md instructs the model to keep the window lean by using `@path/to/file` to reference docs on demand instead of pasting ('never ask me to paste'), and to use Plan Mode (Shift+Tab) before multi-step tasks ('Outline first, execute after approval'). README operationalizes @-refs with worked examples (Read @templates/prd-template.md; Summarize @meeting-notes/standup-03-04.md; Compare @competitor-notes/notion.md against monday.md). This is lazy/just-in-time retrieval via file pointers — a markdown-pointer discipline, not a vector store.

CLAUDE.md Context Management: 'After ~40 exchanges, offer to write a HANDOFF.md (state, decisions, open questions, next steps) and restart' plus 'Suggest /clear when switching between unrelated tasks.' README raises the cap to ~50 ('quality degrades past this') and gives the round-trip protocol: end a session with 'Write a HANDOFF.md', start the next with 'Read @HANDOFF.md and continue', plus `claude --continue` (last session) and `claude --resume` (pick from history). The handoff doc is a manually-triggered continuity journal across /clear boundaries; the cap is a heuristic, not an enforced hook.

CLAUDE.md section: 'When I correct you, immediately propose a rule for this file. Wait for approval before editing'; recurring issues should be proposed as a `.claude/rules/` file 'instead of bloating this file'; and an explicit pruning gate: 'Every rule in this file must earn its place. If removing it wouldn't cause mistakes, it doesn't belong.' README adds a quarterly-prune cadence. A human-in-the-loop capture loop that folds corrections back into the durable config, with an anti-bloat counterweight.

prd-writer/SKILL.md treats the PRD as the durable artifact that evolves through 6 named stages (Team Kickoff speclet -> Planning Review 1-pager -> XFN Kickoff -> Solution Review full PRD -> Launch Readiness specs -> Impact Review post-launch results link). 'You don't write a PRD once. You write it over time... a living document reflecting the team's current thinking at each stage.' Stage 6 explicitly appends a results link at the top to 'close the build-test-learn loop' — a delta-aware re-edit of the same source-of-truth doc rather than a new doc per stage.

Single root rule file with Cursor .mdc frontmatter (description, globs: empty, alwaysApply: true) that is injected into EVERY session. Defines the agent's identity ('strategic product leadership coach, not a generic assistant'), an anti-sycophancy 'Sparring Stance' (take positions, ban hedging words like 'interesting'/'you might consider', demand named personas over categories, flag failure patterns like solution-seeking-problem), output standards (direct tone, tables/frameworks/checklists, no generic templates), and an explicit scope-boundary rule: when a specialized skill like /create-prd or /discovery-researcher activates, defer to its instructions over baseline coaching. Functions as a governance/anti-drift constitution + always-loaded core identity.

The repo IS the context store — a fixed on-disk markdown taxonomy is the durable memory. Per-initiative folder (initiatives/[name]/) holds the full discovery→delivery artifact lifecycle as subfolders: user-interviews/{snapshots,synthesis,transcripts}, opportunities/, assumptions/, solutions/, design/, prototype/, prd/, product-analytics/, tasks/. Each stage writes a markdown file the next stage reads (doc-as-baton, e.g. snapshots → synthesis → opportunities → solutions → assumptions; PRD → tasks). company-level-context/{okrs, product-vision-and-strategy, team-structure} holds always-relevant strategy facts that skills pull from (create-prd reads company-level-context/product-vision-and-strategy/). Folders themselves act as the index of 'where knowledge lives' for retrieval — no vector DB.

The setup-initiative skill bootstraps context by copying the entire initiatives/_templates/initiative-template/ tree into initiatives/[name]/, preserving every subfolder, per-folder README.md, and .gitkeep files (so empty dirs survive git). The root README is customized with initiative details; sub-READMEs are preserved as-is. This is context-scaffolding: a command writes N starter context files into a fixed folder so downstream skills always find the expected slots.

create-prd explicitly enumerates the prior artifacts it consumes as context before drafting: approved one-pager + selected solution from solutions/, validated assumptions from assumptions/, design brief, user research, and company strategy from company-level-context/. The resulting prd-[feature].md becomes the source-of-truth that generate-tasks executes against. Demonstrates the document-handoff chain where each artifact is a typed input slot read by file path.

Pattern_raw_name: ai/memory-bank/ persistent project memory (Senior Project Manager)
How_implemented: The project-manager-senior agent is hardwired to a filesystem memory bank: it READS exact specs from `ai/memory-bank/site-setup.md` (treats the spec as source of truth, quotes it with a 125-char max-quote rule, forbids adding unrequested scope) and WRITES decomposed tasks to `ai/memory-bank/tasks/[project-slug]-tasklist.md` as 30-60min developer tasks with acceptance criteria. The spec file is the durable context baton; the tasklist file is the handoff artifact a developer agent reads next. Memory is plain markdown files on a fixed path convention keyed by project-slug.

Worked example showing agents share context via an MCP memory server exposing remember/recall/rollback/search ops instead of manual copy-paste handoffs. Each deliverable is stored with MULTIPLE TAGS: a project-name tag (`retroboard`), an artifact-type tag (`api-spec`), the producing agent (`backend-architect`), AND the intended RECEIVER agent (`frontend-developer`) so the downstream agent auto-discovers its inputs on `recall` by querying the project name. Rollback replaces manual version tracking when QA fails. This is tag-routed retrieval (receiver-addressed messages) over a memory graph, distinct from a vector RAG.

Pattern_raw_name: Identity & Memory block (per-agent 'You remember and carry forward')
How_implemented: Every agent file opens with a `## 🧠 Identity & Memory` section that bakes durable operating principles directly into the prompt as a 'you remember and carry forward' bulleted creed (e.g. PM: 'Every product decision involves trade-offs—make them explicit', 'momentum is a moat'). This is governance/anti-drift encoded inline rather than in a separate constitution file; convert.sh even splits this header out into a dedicated SOUL.md persona file for the OpenClaw target.

Two root JSON files act as the index of the system: divisions.json maps the 16 divisions to their agents (the roster taxonomy), tools.json holds integration metadata for the 12+ target tools. The installer/desktop app and convert.sh read these as the map of what exists and where it installs.

crew.py instantiates a crewAI TextFileKnowledgeSource over knowledge/rules.txt + knowledge/user_preference.txt. rules.txt is a 9-point 'how to write for an agentic coder' constitution (start small, design before code, ask humans, TDD, guardrails, keep README/design.md current) that the final PRD task is told to obey; user_preference.txt is a tiny always-loaded persona card (name=Jon Doe, Product Leader, AU, interested in AI agents). These are injected as crew knowledge rather than per-agent prompts, so every agent sees the governing rules + user facts.

Each task declares a `context:` list naming the upstream task(s) whose outputs feed it (e.g. design_task context=[features_task, subject_matter_expert_task]; final PRD task context=[purpose,backlog,QA,features,design]). crewAI passes the prior task outputs as in-context input — a doc-as-baton handoff chain where the artifact of step N is the typed input of step N+1, with a fan-in at the final PRD synthesis step.

The terminal task sets output_file: output/product_requirements_document.md; the whole pipeline exists to produce one durable PRD artifact explicitly written 'so it will be used by an agentic coder', i.e. the PRD is the source-of-truth context object that downstream coding agents execute against.

pm-setup command scaffolds a fixed folder of 8 markdown files (product-info, business-metrics, strategic-goals, current-roadmap, tech-stack, customer-segments, team-info, competitive-landscape). Every agent's frontmatter+body instructs 'read .claude/product-context/* first, ask only for gaps.' docs/context-architecture.md formally specifies the files, their owning sections, and three read patterns (direct file read for agents, context-parameter for commands, Phase-0 gather for workflows). strategic-goals.md + current-roadmap.md act as the goal-anchored relevance layer.

Pattern_raw_name: Progressive context-building wizard (pm-setup creates essentials now, journey agents create the rest)
How_implemented: pm-setup deliberately creates only 2 required files up front (product basics + tech stack) and explains that journey-stage agents will write the remaining context files as the user progresses (Stage1 validation -> competitive-landscape.md, Stage2 -> customer-segments.md, Stage5 -> roadmap). Workflows that gather new info write it back to the context files so future runs don't re-ask. --update mode re-prompts each file. Uses AskUserQuestion tool for the interactive elicitation.

context-scanner (Haiku) scans the codebase, presents findings with file-path EVIDENCE + high/medium/low confidence scores, gets user validation, then WRITES validated facts into context files with '_Auto-discovered: [date] / _Last validated: [date] / _Evidence:' provenance metadata. It explicitly 'does NOT return JSON to other agents' — invoking PM agents 'read the updated context files after control returns,' so the scan's raw output never re-enters the caller's window (context firewall via the filesystem rather than via a summary).

Pattern_raw_name: Structured Phase-0 -> Phase-N CONTEXT payload passing
How_implemented: context-architecture.md prescribes a CONTEXT object assembled in Phase 0 (product/metrics/goals/roadmap/team) and then injected into EVERY Task() subagent prompt as a labeled block (**Product**, **Business Metrics**, **Strategic Goals**, **Previous Phase Output**), so each specialist receives both broad product context AND the prior phase's artifact. Team commands implement this literally: each teammate prompt embeds Phase-1 product-context excerpts and, in cross-examination, the other agents' full output reports.

Pattern_raw_name: Per-agent memory: blocks (memory: project / memory: local)
How_implemented: Most agents declare `memory: project` (context-scanner uses `memory: local`) in frontmatter and have an explicit '## Memory' section: 'Before starting work: read your memory for prior routing patterns / spec preferences'; 'After completing: save which specialist was routed to, decisions, journey state'; with a self-consolidation rule 'if memory exceeds 200 lines, consolidate older entries.' This is an end-of-task capture loop persisted per-agent across sessions.

Pattern_raw_name: requirements-engineer reads context files to inform PRD-complexity scoring
How_implemented: Before picking a PRD template the agent reads tech-stack.md / product-info.md / current-features.md / strategic-goals.md to inform a 0-10 score on 3 dimensions (e.g. 'microservices in tech-stack.md adds +2 technical'). Context is loaded just-in-time to drive a downstream decision rather than dumped wholesale.

After a validation sprint the lead offers to save the final verdict to .claude/product-context/validation-[idea-slug].md 'available to other PM agents for future reference' — closing the loop so an ephemeral multi-agent debate becomes durable shared context.

A single hooks.json wires Claude Code lifecycle events to node/python scripts in hooks/scripts/. SessionStart runs session-start.js + context-loader.js (loads prior context, git info, configs, pending tasks). PostToolUse(Bash) runs suggest-compact.js (tracks edits, recommends compaction). PreCompact runs pre-compact.js (preserves critical context before compaction). SessionEnd runs session-end.js (persists context) + learning-log.js (records session insights and commits them) — an explicit end-of-session capture loop. PreToolUse guards (secret-scanner, commit-guard, smart-approve) are governance, not context.

A dedicated agent persona whose entire job is token-budget discipline. Prescribes a numeric budget allocation (critically-modified files 40%, reference material 20%, skip generated/vendor dirs entirely), progressive loading (summaries first, details on demand), and aggressive compaction (collapse stable sections, archive decisions). For inter-agent handoff it specifies a context-firewall-style briefing contract: 'Provide a task-specific briefing, not a full conversation dump' containing objective, relevant file paths, key constraints, expected output format — explicitly EXCLUDING debugging history, rejected approaches, tangents. Persists architectural decisions via CLAUDE.md across sessions.

Pattern_raw_name: contexts/ mode files (dev.md, debug.md, deploy.md, research.md, review.md)
How_implemented: Five frontmatter-less markdown 'context' files, each a short mode-specific standards doc (dev.md has Behavior / Coding / Tools / Avoid sections — e.g. 'Write working code first, optimize later', 'never push to main directly'). They are reference standards a user pastes/loads to put Claude into a posture (dev vs debug vs review), not auto-loaded tiered context. A lightweight registers-as-mode variant of a root instruction file.

A SKILL.md (frontmatter: author awrshift, version 3.2.0, tags [memory,context-management]) describing a two-layer memory architecture — a 'hot cache' for frequently accessed info plus a wiki-style knowledge base using internal wikilinks. Project isolation via separate backlogs; safety hooks protect against context degradation during compaction; a /close-day command does end-of-day synthesis consolidating the day into structured memory; /tour gives orientation. Vendored into the catalog as one of 35 skill folders.

Every agent's system prompt embeds a fixed JSON 'context query' template the agent is told to emit at invocation to a (notional) context-manager before acting, e.g. product-manager.md: {"requesting_agent":"product-manager","request_type":"get_product_context","payload":{"query":"Product context needed: vision, target users, market landscape, business model, current metrics, and growth objectives."}}. project-manager uses get_project_context (objectives/scope/timeline/budget/resources/stakeholders/success criteria); business-analyst get_business_context; scrum-master an agile_context_query. The payload.query is a single natural-language string enumerating exactly the facts the role needs — a typed, role-scoped pull request for context rather than dumping the whole project window in. It is a prompt convention, not a runtime protocol (no schema/validator ships).

Pattern_raw_name: AGENTS.md root file (CLAUDE.md symlinked to it)
How_implemented: Single hand-authored root context file at repo root; CLAUDE.md is a symlink to AGENTS.md, and Gemini loads it via .gemini/settings.json — one identity/rules file feeding all harnesses. Sets naming rules (lowercase-hyphen, '__' reserved for adapter namespacing), authoring invariant ('all authoring under plugins/<name>/'), tooling mandate (uv/ruff/ty, forbids pip/mypy/black), and 4 commit gates. Explicitly framed as 'a map, not an encyclopedia.'

Pattern_raw_name: ~150-line context cap + progressive disclosure to docs/ and skill references/
How_implemented: Convention (enforced by make garden) that root/context files stay under ~150 lines and skill bodies under ~8KB; procedural detail offloads to skills, deep reference to docs/. Codex adapter caps body at 8KB and spills overflow into references/; Gemini uses @{path} injection for large bodies — progressive disclosure baked into the generator, not just docs.

plugin_eval/corpus.py maintains a persisted JSON index of all skills (CorpusEntry: name, path, category, line_count, elo_rating). init_from_source() walks plugins/*/skills/*/SKILL.md to build it; select_references() filters by category/line_count and returns entries nearest a baseline Elo to use as comparison anchors during evaluation. A map-of-where-skills-live used for retrieval/calibration, no vector DB.

generate.py emits a global aggregation pass (adapter.emit_global()) producing shared manifests (marketplace.json for Claude Code, .cursor-plugin manifests, etc.) that index every plugin — the discovery/index layer each harness reads to know what plugins exist and how to load them.

Pattern_raw_name: marketplace.json directory (Tier-2 index of where plugins live)
How_implemented: A single .claude-plugin/marketplace.json holds a flat plugins[] array of 243 entries; each entry is a pointer, not content — a name+description+category plus a source object that is either a local path ("./plugins/x"), a {source:"url"|"git-subdir", url, path, ref, sha} git reference, or a github source. Claude Code reads this index to discover/install; the full plugin (skills, agents, MCP) is fetched just-in-time on install from the referenced repo at a pinned sha. So the marketplace is a map of where capability lives, resolved lazily — no plugin bodies are inlined.

Ships a split pair: claude-md-improver (a model-invoked SKILL that audits existing CLAUDE.md files against current codebase state and scores their quality, triggered by codebase changes / 'audit my CLAUDE.md') and /revise-claude-md (a user command that, at end of session, captures session learnings back into CLAUDE.md). Treats the root instruction file as a living, LLM-maintained KB with an explicit periodic-audit + end-of-session capture loop rather than a static doc.

The /code-review command treats the repo's CLAUDE.md as the governing spec for the review: a Haiku agent first returns the file PATHS (not contents) of the root CLAUDE.md plus any CLAUDE.md in modified dirs; one of the five review agents audits the diff specifically for CLAUDE.md adherence; and the confidence-scoring pass double-checks that a flagged style issue is actually called out in CLAUDE.md before keeping it. The instruction doc becomes the source of truth the reviewers execute against.

A repo-root CONNECTORS.md per plugin documents a 3-column table (Category | ~~placeholder | included MCP servers + alternatives, e.g. ~~calendar, ~~chat, ~~tracker, ~~analytics, ~~design, ~~feedback). Every SKILL.md refers to tools abstractly ('pull from ~~tracker', 'post via ~~chat') rather than naming Linear/Jira; CONNECTORS.md is the index that resolves a placeholder to whatever the user actually connected. synthesize-research/SKILL.md explicitly tells the agent to 'check CONNECTORS.md for unfamiliar placeholders' — so it functions as a just-in-time lookup map of where capability lives, decoupling workflow prose from concrete vendor IDs.

Per-plugin .mcp.json declares mcpServers as remote type:http endpoints (mcp.linear.app/mcp, mcp.atlassian.com/v1/mcp, mcp.notion.com/mcp, mcp.figma.com/mcp, mcp.amplitude.com/mcp + an amplitude-eu region variant, app.pendo.io/mcp, mcp.intercom.com/mcp, api.fireflies.ai/mcp, similarweb, slack with an inline oauth clientId+callbackPort block). Gmail/Google-Calendar entries ship with empty url strings (placeholders the user fills). External system state lives behind these connectors and is fetched at runtime by ID/query, never copied into repo context.

Documented verbatim in skill-creator: (1) Metadata = name+description always in context (~100 words); (2) SKILL.md body loaded only when the skill triggers (<500 lines ideal); (3) bundled resources (scripts/, references/, assets/) loaded as-needed, and scripts can EXECUTE without ever being read into context. This is the literal Tier-1/2/3 tree: frontmatter is the always-loaded preamble, SKILL.md is the on-trigger map, reference files are JIT content.

Pattern_raw_name: references/ as JIT context files with read-pointers + TOC rule
How_implemented: Skills split deep docs into references/*.md that SKILL.md points to with explicit 'read this file when X' guidance rather than inlining. Rule: for large reference files (>300 lines) include a table of contents; SKILL.md stays a map. mcp-builder ships reference/{python_mcp_server.md,node_mcp_server.md,mcp_best_practices.md,evaluation.md} and docx keeps the heavy XML/redlining detail in scripts/office/ helpers — Claude reads only the relevant variant. Domain-organization pattern: SKILL.md holds workflow+selection, references/{aws,gcp,azure}.md hold the just-in-time branch.

Pattern_raw_name: Document-handoff workspace for the eval loop
How_implemented: skill-creator writes a fixed artifact tree as a sibling to the skill: <skill>-workspace/iteration-N/eval-<id>/{with_skill,without_skill}/outputs/, plus eval_metadata.json, timing.json, grading.json, benchmark.json/.md, feedback.json. Each stage reads the prior stage's file (executor outputs -> grader's grading.json -> aggregate_benchmark.py -> analyzer notes -> generate_review.py viewer -> human feedback.json -> next iteration). The doc IS the baton between subagent stages; iteration-N+1 reads iteration-N via --previous-workspace.

Test runs are executed in fresh subagent contexts (one per eval, spawned in the same turn), each saving only its outputs + a small timing.json/metrics.json back to the workspace; the heavy transcript stays in the subagent. The main session reads compact JSON summaries (grading.json, benchmark.json), not the raw run logs — keeping the orchestrator context lean across many parallel runs.
